87709117.title: Tachikoma
summary: Jun comes home to find the apartment overrun by foot-tall Tachikoma spider bots that Motoko built as an auto-loot and surveillance system. The little bots call her "Major," have started an automated factory in the rented apartment below, and one has gone full philosopher after reading too many of Misty's texts — the others drag it into a cupboard shrine to shut it up.

88013495.title: Collected Snippets (to page 101) #1
summary: A compilation of reader jokes and one-liners from the first hundred pages of the thread — riffs on gamer system notifications, Motoko's ninja explanations, Pokémon-style Adam Smasher jokes, and other community in-jokes. Not a narrative sidestory.

88052428.title: SHARD: CounterIntel Investigation
summary: A counterintel agent named Jenkins demands a briefing on the clusterfuck, and his analyst V explains that the suspect — "Mokoto Kusanagi, age 14" — is obviously using a forged identity ripped from an obscure old comic. No fourteen-year-old could have pulled this off.

88057372.title: SHARD: CounterIntel Investigation 2
summary: Continuation of the counterintel briefing — the analysts dig deeper into Motoko's identity and operations, growing more baffled that the profile keeps pointing back to a teenager who shouldn't exist on paper.

88130680.title: SHARD: CounterIntel Investigation 3
summary: The investigation deepens as the counterintel team cross-references Motoko's growing list of impossible feats against her completely clean — suspiciously clean — public record. Every lead circles back to the same conclusion nobody wants to accept: she really is just some kid from Kabuki.

88251769.title: The 'Talk'
summary: After dropping Motoko off post-Scav-den, Hiromi and Ichi hash it out with Malcolm and Omaeda over group chat. Ichi worries Motoko has changed too much; Hiromi shuts it down — she's not cyberpsycho, just a girl who woke up from a coma and needed to break something. Malcolm's already eyeing the next score, Omaeda's cautiously in, and Hiromi declares the matter settled. Meanwhile, somewhere in the Badlands, Motoko bleeds into the sand during a storm — so much for resting at home.

88294543.title: Scorpion's Angel of Death
summary: Told from the Aldecaldos' perspective — Mitch, Cassidy, and the family arrive at the Wraith camp Motoko cleared and find every last Raffen dead, most taken out before they knew what hit them. Flashback to the camp alarm: a purple-haired, bandaged teenage girl rolls up in a Raffen-marked car with a half-conscious Scorpion, rattles off a spec-ops-calm sitrep to Saul, hands over the camp coordinates as payment, claims the leader's car as her fee, and drives back to Night City before anyone can stop her. The family hauls home a half-dozen vehicles, guns, and Scorpion's Apollo bike — and Mitch figures they haven't seen the last of their young solo.
88294543.kill_notes: No kills depicted on-screen — events reference Motoko's prior raid.

88316995.title: Back Alley Saint
summary: Viktor installs a donated cyberarm on a kid named Jaime who couldn't afford one — part of the mountain of Scav chrome Motoko handed over with orders to help as many people as possible. Vik keeps a photo wall of every success case, dozens of smiling faces with new limbs and restored organs. Misty notes the girl's aura is ancient and displaced, like it doesn't belong in this world, and Vik quietly resolves to pay back his debt to the teenage solo who keeps adding to it.

88317061.title: Tank Chuckin'
summary: A what-if vignette imagining endgame Motoko at max Body — she strolls through a compound shrugging off every round, catches a rocket out of the air and crushes it, dodges a tank shell by tilting her head, then sinks her fingers into thirty tons of armor plating and tosses the vehicle aside like tissue paper. The narrator watches his own death approach with something close to peace before an invisible blade takes his head.
88317061.kill_notes: No real kills — speculative what-if scenario.

88317205.title: Ghost in the City: What if?
summary: The original post that spawned the Tank Chuckin' snippet — a reader speculates on what happens when Motoko pushes past Body 20 without chrome, imagining a 1.4-meter girl casually flipping tanks like a Resident Evil Tyrant.

88317529.title: Ghost in the City: What if? Part 2
summary: Seras writes a continuation of the What-If scenario — Motoko gets hauled in by authorities and plays dumb over free donuts, baffling her interrogators while casually admitting to feats that shouldn't be physically possible for an unchromed teenager.

88325200.title: HMG-Chan
summary: Told from the perspective of Motoko's HMG — the gun narrates its joyous rebirth from rusting elevator guard to beloved companion, rhapsodizing about the dakka lords and Mother Deuce as it shreds a Raffen vehicle. Then a crash buries it in sand, and its wielder is gone. It waits, certain She will come back.
88325200.kill_notes: No kills counted — gun POV vignette.

88333063.title: We are born of the weeb.
summary: Motoko discovers Night City's century of divergent media and falls down a binge-watching hole with Hiromi — including a weebified Star Wars prequel trilogy where Amidala is Japanese and Jar Jar looks like a kappa. She finds a cult-classic 2037 magical girl anime, gets obsessed enough to have Wakako source merch, and receives a mysterious delivery box she can't figure out how to open.

88333930.title: Motoko X Hiromi "Just Roommates"
summary: A future-fic where Hiromi is an Arasaka exec and Motoko is Night City's most feared solo — Hiromi dramatically recounts how Motoko princess-carried her out of a corpo kidnapping, while Motoko insists it wasn't that impressive. Fixers have apparently added a "Motoko Clause" letting edgerunners bail on jobs if she's on the other side. The bickering ends when Jun texts about dinner.

88334851.title: Motoko Greatest Scientific Genius in the Universe!
summary: A brief meta snippet — a reader riffs on the Motoko-as-exec-girlfriend premise from the previous omake, echoing the same future where Hiromi climbs Arasaka and Motoko racks up an unbroken mission record.

88346338.title: Pretty Solo Merc Sailor Scout Motoko
summary: Motoko gets a time-critical mission and shows up in full Sailor Moon cosplay — mini-skirted sailor fuku, tiara and all. She announces herself as "Pretty Solo Merc Sailor Scout Motoko" to a stunned Jun and a squad of Maelstrom gangers, drops all of them in two seconds with quickdraw, then double-taps every body "for good measure."

88346644.title: Fighting Scavs by Moonlight
summary: A Sailor Moon parody song rewriting the theme as Motoko's anthem — fighting Scavs by moonlight, looting chrome by daylight, never running from a real fight. Short comedic snippet riffing on the Sailor Scout Motoko premise.

88353349.title: Don't get between Hiromi and her sword
summary: An alternate take on the Adam Smasher fight — David wastes his chance by turning it into a twenty-minute chrome-measuring boxing match instead of using Motoko's sword properly. Hiromi shows up on a bike in a cyber-ninja suit, picks up the discarded blade, and cuts down both David's borged-out opponents in seconds. Rebecca starts wanting her own preem blade, and David earns the title of "Gonk who could've defeated Adam Smasher."
88353349.kill_notes: No canonical kills — alternate timeline what-if.

88357489.title: Omae Wa Mou Shinderu
summary: A Scav crew spots what they think is an easy mark in the Corpo district — a lone girl without visible chrome or bodyguards. As they rush her, Motoko turns with a cheeky smile and draws her katana. One soft *click* of the blade resheathing is the last thing Alexei hears before everything falls apart.
88357489.kill_notes: No kills explicitly confirmed — implied via iaijutsu.

88357756.title: The season peaked at episode 17.
summary: Continuation of the Scav ambush from Omae Wa Mou Shinderu — the aftermath plays out like an anime episode as witnesses try to process what they just saw. A comment on how the best moments in Night City always end too quickly.

88369507.title: Drive thru Anime
summary: Motoko takes Hiromi to a drive-in theater to force anime on her, only to find V and Rogue already there on Johnny's date night. Motoko casually greets Rogue and the resurrected rockerboy while Hiromi short-circuits at being in the same parking lot as the queen of the Afterlife.

88370710.title: A Little Toy Of Scrap Metal
summary: A Tachikoma-focused snippet where the little spider bots deal with a logic virus — riffing on the earlier Tachikoma omake and exploring the budding AI personalities Motoko accidentally created in her workshop projects.

88372006.title: Tip over
summary: A micro-snippet joking about Hiromi jumping into Motoko's car — barely a sentence of content, more of a one-liner reaction to the main story.

88379113.title: The Brave little HMG
summary: HMG-Chan sequel — the gun wakes up alone in the desert, half-buried in sand, and tries to drag itself toward civilization on its little gas-tube arms. It digs holes, monologues about its ancestry from Mother Deuce and Uncle Kalashnikov, and stubbornly refuses to accept that the Major is gone. Equal parts heartbreaking and absurd.
88379113.kill_notes: No kills — sentient gun survival story.

88382173.title: Motoko Kusanagi, Teenage Mother
summary: Motoko rebuilds HMG-Chan from scratch — new fluted barrel, titanium components, custom magazine system using gutted Defender mags refitted for fifty-cal rounds. She treats the project like restoring a beloved child, sparing no expense and adding a rubber butt-pad so she can shoulder-fire it solo.

88383250.title: You saved them!
summary: Motoko sprints through a three-way gang battle between Tyger Claws, Scavs, and Maelstrom — not to fight, but to save a wounded bystander. She rips electronics from a vending machine, guts a charging Scav for spare chrome, and performs improvised surgery using food-cart tubing and syrupy sauces as IVs. Hiromi finds her hours later, half-asleep on the street, still cradling her patient.
88383250.kill_notes: 1 kill: Scav who charged her, impaled with a drain pipe.

88385182.title: HMG-Chan stories
summary: A collection of short HMG-Chan snippets expanding the sentient gun's lore — more adventures of the heavy machine gun's burgeoning personality and its devotion to the Major.

88386232.title: Tools of the trade
summary: A riff on Motoko's crafting and tech skills — focusing on how she approaches weapon and cyberware modification with a mix of gamer min-maxing logic and genuine mechanical talent.

88388929.title: Motoko wants the first drink
summary: Rogue is enjoying a quiet day at the Afterlife when a small, blood-soaked, lightly smoking girl drags what used to be Adam Smasher's mangled borg chassis across the bar floor. Motoko parks the twisted metal corpse and orders a drink — the first one named after her.
88388929.kill_notes: No kills on-screen — aftermath of implied Smasher fight.

88390222.title: Jun's Diary Shard
summary: Jun's private diary entries running parallel to the main story — he processes Motoko's transformation from his coma-survivor little sister into someone who raids Scav dens solo, worries about the Tyger Claws finding out, and wrestles with big-brother guilt while quietly being proud of how strong she's become.
88390222.kill_notes: No kills — Jun POV diary entries.

88396432.title: Fast as fuck boy!
summary: A short snippet riffing on the Body stat discussion — imagining what happens when Motoko's overall fitness hits absurd levels, making her impossibly fast despite never specifically training for speed.

88406587.title: What Rests Within A Blade?
summary: Hiromi enters a kendo tournament using the stance Motoko taught her — Te Ura Gasumi No Kamae, a guard nobody at the dojo recognizes. Through the bout she flashes back to lessons with the old Motoko before the coma, revealing the depth of their friendship and how much Hiromi channels her best friend's teachings into the blade.

88411618.title: Didn't get them all
summary: Continuation of the kendo piece — Hiromi reflects on the gap between tournament fighting and street-level reality, acknowledging that technique alone won't keep you alive when the chrome comes out.

88413301.title: Netchatting
summary: Omaeda works his ancient cyberdeck in Megabuilding H8, chatting with a mentor netrunner in an encrypted room. They sift through the fried data from the Scav den Motoko blew through — most of it torched, but enough scraps to hint at larger networks.

88420837.title: 7 Years Ago
summary: A flashback to seven years before the story — glimpsing the Kusanagi family and the world Motoko inhabited before the coma changed everything. Sets the emotional baseline for what was lost.

88427838.title: Lightning and the Thunder
summary: A Jun-focused piece running parallel to the main story — Jun fights in the corporate war while Motoko tears through Night City's underworld. The contrast between his disciplined soldiering and her chaotic edgerunning plays out across matched scenes.
88427838.kill_notes: No kills attributed — Jun combat scenes.

88430847.title: THIS IS NOT CANNON
summary: A deliberately non-canon crack snippet riffing on Motoko's relationship with Viktor — exaggerating her habit of showing up with impossible chrome donations until Vik's debt spirals into absurdity.

88431651.title: Drunk Hiromi
summary: Hiromi gets wasted and says things she'd never admit sober — probably about Motoko, probably embarrassing, definitely loud. Short comedic vignette.

88438317.title: Badass Weekly'
summary: A fictional Night City magazine profiles Motoko's exploits — written as an in-universe article cataloging the impossible feats of a teenage solo that nobody in the media business quite believes but can't stop covering.

88442601.title: The Return Of The Laughing Man
summary: Riffs on the Ghost in the Shell Laughing Man motif — someone in Night City starts noticing that Motoko's netrunning signature bears an eerie resemblance to a legend from old anime, and nobody gets the reference except her.

88446558.title: No one else
summary: Wakako rattles off a list of available agents for Motoko's new team — combat specialist Kenpachi Zaraki, sniper Usagi Tsukino, generalist Mihoshi Kuramitsu, netrunner Light Yagami, and demolitions duo Megumin and Lina Inverse. The catch: no one else will work with any of them.

88469370.title: Here we go again
summary: An alternate-universe Female V — a Nomad with a hybrid nano-forge body and deployable armor — storms a border patrol station on foot while Jackie Welles watches in disbelief from the passenger seat. V tears through armed security with her Linear Frame, opens the border, and disables the comms array before calmly retracting her combat shell back into what looks like a normal Nomad body. Jackie and V form a partnership despite the chaos, and less than a week later she meets another V — a fellow product of the failed Virtuous Citizen Project.
88469370.kill_notes: V kills several border patrol but this is an AU snippet

88495311.title: Wakako's Notes
summary: Wakako's internal file on Motoko Kusanagi: two gigs completed, high satisfaction rating, suspected of wiping out the scav den in H1, efficient solo with excellent stealth and improvisational weapon skills. The final note flags Motoko as a bro-con who ran dangerous gigs just to get a minute-by-minute breakdown of Jun's day.

88495926.title: "Oops."
summary: File appears to be a duplicate of Wakako's Notes (index 43) with surrounding forum discussion about Motoko's parry perk and adaptation mechanics. No distinct sidestory content beyond what is covered in the previous entry.

88498053.title: Netdeck is mightier
summary: Someone confronts Motoko about beating a gonk over the head with their own netdeck instead of using their mantis blades. Motoko insists she "used what she had" and drops the pun: "The Netdeck is mightier than the sword."

88500711.title: SIBLING RIVALRY
summary: A comedy skit laying out the Kusanagi sibling dynamic: Jun and Motoko mirror each other line for line — both claiming to be the responsible one, the cool badass, and the engine of vengeance — before simultaneously yelling at each other to stop copying.

88513683.title: Yandere
summary: Jun wakes up strapped to his own bed with Motoko sitting at his side, staring at him with empty eyes. She explains in a flat monotone that since onii-chan kept running off to do dangerous things and never answering her calls, she had no choice — he can't run without legs and arms. Jun looks down and finds his cyberlimbs detached and set on a workbench across the room, guarded by an HMG.

88514931.title: I'm Alive
summary: Explores how "Still Alive" became a greeting among Tyger Claws fighting the Maelstrom war, echoing the Mandalorian "Su cuy'gar." Jun trades the phrase with Kenzo after sniping a Maelstrom borg, hears it at a wake for fallen comrade Ishida, and gets advice from Kakashi to stop ghosting Motoko before she does something stupid. Haunted by memories of war — assassinations, civilian collateral, shooting a kid Motoko's age — Jun can only muster two words to his sister: "I'm alive."

88515471.title: Yandere Part 2
summary: A meta-commentary connecting Jun's terse "I'm alive" text to the Mandalorian greeting, followed by a continuation showing Jun in the thick of the Maelstrom war — pinned down by a borg's HMG fire, sniping it from a rooftop on Kenzo's call, trading "Still Alive" with his squad. At Ishida's wake, Kakashi warns Jun not to let OPSEC silence become a weapon against his own sister. Jun tries to write a farewell letter but can't finish before Kakashi short-circuits an entire Maelstrom APC with a touch. In the end, all Jun sends Motoko is: "I'm alive."

88532349.title: Is Shounen Jump the Big Bad?
summary: Jun drags Motoko to a Tyger Claw izakaya to meet his war buddies, and she nearly chokes on sake when she realizes the entire crew are walking anime trademark violations — Hatake Kakashi, Luffy, Kurosaki Ichigo, Yagami Light, the Elric brothers, Natsu Dragneel. After carrying a drunk Jun home, Motoko puts her head in her hands and wonders aloud if she's trapped in a multi-cross and whether Shounen Jump is the final boss.

88539357.title: The Ghost Sightings: Episode #14
summary: Kaname Hayato wakes strapped to a ripperdoc chair in a scav den, memories of being drugged and grabbed flooding back. A new hire named Yami records a BD of the operation for management, meeting the crew: Duncan the boss, Bear the four-armed cook who makes suspiciously good "pork" meatloaf, and netrunner Kathryn. The episode walks through the scav den's internal workings from the naive BD-recorder's perspective, laying bare the mundane horror of a chrome-harvesting operation run like a business.

88549776.title: Cali-fornia Girls!
summary: Jun comes home early and catches Motoko in a full leopard-print outfit dancing to "California Girls" in front of a mirror. She shrieks, cries in embarrassment, and hurls a stuffed tiger, a robot action figure, and a pigtailed doll at him. Jun stands frozen for several minutes, silently questioning whether his sister needs new friends.

88551222.title: SHARD: CounterIntel Investigation 4
summary: A netrunner named Koga hired by Wakako briefs Jun on a corpo experiment involving coma patients and the Collective Unconscious — explaining that his sister's brain was too damaged to recover normally, and the Motoko who woke up is essentially running on a previous life's memories. Jun doesn't care about the metaphysics; she's still his sister. Then Koga drops the real bombshell: Motoko now works for Wakako as an edgerunner. Jun's shout can be heard around the block.

88564029.title: HMG-Chans long day
summary: Narrated from HMG-Chan's perspective: Motoko buckles the battered heavy machine gun into the passenger seat, drives to the dump for parts, then brings her home for a full cleaning. HMG-Chan meets Unity (Motoko's sassy first pistol) and Burya (the silent, terrifying Burya revolver who rumbles cryptic Russian). The gun blisses out during maintenance, nearly moans aloud, and drifts to sleep calling Motoko "Momtoko."

88566102.title: Collective Unconscious
summary: A netrunner named Koga explains to Jun that a corpo experiment linked to the Collective Unconscious may have given multiple people past-life flashes — including Jun himself, who remembers seeing himself as Briareos for a split second. Koga's theory: the original Motoko died in the scav den and was replaced by a previous-life memory backup. Jun accepts it without hesitation. Then Koga casually asks if he knows his sister is now working as an edgerunner for Wakako, and Jun's outraged scream echoes through the block.

88581636.title: The Briar Rose
summary: Yuri grows up raised by his superhuman big sister — a bartender at the Afterlife who punches holes in walls and works for Rogue on the side. She gives him a flechette-loaded Carnage shotgun called Briar Rose, and he takes it into Maelstrom. On his first delivery run he splits three scavs in half with one shot. Yuri rises fast through the gang, modding Briar Rose into a monster, until the Tyger Claw war consumes him. His sister, meanwhile, marries Loid Forger and sends him a bandolier as a gift from his new family.
88581636.kill_notes: Yuri kills scavs but this is an OC-focused side story

88585314.title: Richard Guyson
summary: File contains a repeat of The Briar Rose (index 56) content. The actual "Richard Guyson" story appears to be the Ghost Sightings Episode #1/#6/#7 content about Putra, a shopkeeper grieving his husband Rafiq who was killed by scavs, which follows in the next entry.

88586841.title: The Ghost Sightings: Episode #11
summary: A BD-recording newbie named Yami gets hired by scav boss Duncan to document their chrome-harvesting operation for management. He meets Bear, the four-armed cook whose "pork" meatloaf is suspiciously delicious, and Kathryn the overworked netrunner. Through Yami's naive eyes the scav den's daily routine — butchering victims, delivering meals to captives, monitoring security feeds — plays out with banal corporate efficiency, all while the kid just wants to do a good job and earn his paycheck for a Kusanagi bike.

88597629.title: cutie-wootie bullet-shooties
summary: A teary-eyed Motoko walks into a gun shop with a knapsack full of broken HMGs she can't afford to keep, describing each one like an orphaned pet — a metal pretzel that fell out a window, a double-barrel pastafarian, a baby chaingun, and a pair of stabby bayonet-HMGs. The shopkeeper sighs and offers 200 eddies to "adopt" them all.

88599261.title: WANTED: BRRRR
summary: A multi-take comedy: Motoko gets her first gig from Regina to clear a scav den, loads up with Burya, Briar Rose, Unity, and Knife-kun, then sneaks inside ninja-style — only to trip on a corpse. Every den she hits has already been cleaned out by a mystery assassin (later revealed to be a Spy x Family crossover with Yor Forger), leaving Motoko with nothing to shoot. She's forced to pivot to stealth and netrunning gigs, but those get stolen too — by Loid and Anya Forger respectively.

88603443.title: The Ghost Sightings: Episodes #1, #6 and #7
summary: Three Ghost Sightings episodes woven together: Episode #1 shows a scav named Leskov getting his throat slit by a purple-haired girl mid-harvest. Episodes #6 and #7 follow Putra, a grieving shopkeeper whose husband Rafiq was killed by scavs. Putra spirals through grief — delivering scrap toys to orphans, contemplating suicide — until a BD of the Ghost's raid on the scav den that killed Rafiq gives him closure. The Ghost unknowingly saved a man's life without ever meeting him.

88619202.title: Daemon Summoner
summary: Putra, the shopkeeper mourning his scav-murdered husband Rafiq, slots a BD someone pressed into his palm at the market. Inside it he experiences Episode #1 of The Ghost Sightings — a scav named Leskov getting his throat cut by a purple-haired girl mid-harvest. Watching the people who killed Rafiq get slaughtered gives Putra the first spark of something other than grief he's felt in months.

88637532.title: Gamer Exe 1.6.1
summary: An AU where Motoko's body is a Gemini full-body conversion running a Soul Killer engram, puppeted by a doll program to act normal for Jun. Hiromi slots a breaching shard that crashes the doll software, and the Gamer system boots up in its place — leaving engram-Motoko trapped in an artificial body, struggling to feel anything at all. Her first emotion: a distant sadness at Humanity Level 2.

88639479.title: FIRESTARTER
summary: File contains a repeat of Gamer Exe 1.6.1 (index 63). The actual FIRESTARTER content — a Motoko raid on a Maelstrom garage — begins later in the file, where she snaps her fingers to deploy daemons that kill the lights, then hunts panicking gangers in total darkness while a Maelstrom named FrostGuard realizes their netrunner is already dead.

88687587.title: Hiromi Ittō-ryū
summary: Part 2 of Kensei Hiromi. Hiromi slots a Jigen-ryu kenjutsu training sim and grinds through 5,214 practice swings to internalize the school's doctrine: the first strike is everything, a second strike is not even to be considered. The piece breaks down how traditional Japanese sword styles translate into the cyberpunk setting, with Hiromi adapting the one-sword kill philosophy into her own Itto-ryu variant.

88703109.title: HMG-Chan and friends.
summary: HMG-Chan wakes up to Unity bickering with Carnage — a foul-mouthed Budget Arms shotgun — over who belongs in Motoko's loadout. Later she meets Tactician, a Constitutional Arms M2038 who refuses to call Motoko "owner" on Second Amendment grounds and insists on "client." Through the guns' chatter, the apartment's growing arsenal takes on a life of its own: Unity talks trash, Carnage screams profanity, Burya looms in silent menace, and HMG-Chan just wants to dream about shooting scavs with Motoko.

88707948.title: It Runs In The Family
summary: Jun calls Keichi at 5 AM asking for trucks in Maelstrom territory. Keichi rounds up a nervous crew and drives deep into hostile turf, expecting a trap. Instead they find Jun alone in a warehouse surrounded by stolen Maelstrom cargo — weapons, chrome, supplies. Jun's only explanation: "Not anymore" when told it's Strom turf. Keichi helps move the loot but resolves that after this, the two of them need to talk about Jun's accelerating descent.

88710789.title: Oni No Monogatari
summary: Jun flatlines a Maelstrom civilian relative as retribution for the strom attack near Motoko's apartment, then orders his kohai to arrange the severed heads in a traditional kubi-jikken ceremony and spray his Oni sigil. On the ride back, suppressed voices flood his mind — Kasumi's death, his parents, Motoko. The piece tracks Jun's internal war between the vengeance-fueled Oni persona and the brother who still cares.

88740171.title: War Runs
summary: Ichi and Malcolm run supply logistics for the Tyger Claws during the Maelstrom war, hauling ammo, meds, and guns through back streets to frontline strongpoints. They discuss how Motoko's brother told her to sit this one out, Hiromi's locked down by her corpo mom, and Omaeda bailed entirely. The ironic kicker: they wonder what idiot would go out alone on a night like this.

88759521.title: Extraction plan
summary: Motoko grinds crafting and netrunning to build a Knight Rider-style AI car — armored, self-driving, with HMG-Chan mounted in a pop-up turret. The AI boots up speaking like HK-47: "Eager statement: Point me at the meatbags that are in need of immediate aggressive ventilation." Motoko names it Murdercar and they roll out for revenge.

88759848.title: Ichi needs to vent.
summary: Jun drowns in guilt at a bar after Motoko's kidnapping. Ichi, trying to vent his own stress, accidentally rattles off Motoko's entire rap sheet — soloing scav dens, stealing an HMG, giving loot to orphans, massacring Wraiths with a screwdriver through one guy's brain, dropkicking Maelstrom off a building, stealing cars and shotguns. Jun has no idea about any of it. Ichi realizes he just said way too much and silently drains his entire glass.

88761579.title: Ghost in the City The RPG experience.
summary: A comedic meta-retelling of Motoko's kidnapping arc as a tabletop RPG session gone wrong. Jun's player made Perception his dump stat, so he missed every clue that Motoko was grinding combat skills instead of being a proper moe-blob dependent. The GM's kidnapping arc derails when Motoko refuses to scream during torture, the cyberpsycho ripper gains humanity points from being creeped out, and Jun's party spends twelve hours stacking prep bonuses before flatting every borg in the building. Jun's idiot party members then try to steal Motoko's guns.

88763973.title: Motokoium
summary: Motoko declares she can break physics with enough crafting levels and invents element 147, "Motokoium," whose only stable isotope goes nuclear with a nearby explosion. Hiromi immediately asks if you can make a bullet out of it. Motoko considers railgun applications and Hiromi starts hyperventilating.

88774020.title: Gaia Gamer
summary: An AU origin where Motoko's sword was shattered by scavs with gorilla arms, who then stripped her chrome and injected her with the "Essence of Gaia" — a virus that forces out cyberware. The trauma leaves her equating her broken self with her broken blade. When more scavs try to finish her off, the Gamer system manifests Technopathy and a lightning-blast skill called "Nooo!" that fries two of them. Hiromi watches from an alley, now unsure if the Motoko returned to Jun is the real one.

88775247.title: Fanfic-Ception!
summary: File contains a repeat of Gaia Gamer (index 74). The "Fanfic-Ception" title refers to the meta nature of the AU — a fanfic within a fanfic exploring an alternate Gamer origin for Motoko involving the Essence of Gaia and technopathic powers.

88786602.title: Laugh of progress
summary: Motoko monologues about synthesizing Motokoium while Hiromi threatens to leave out of boredom. A Man vs. Machine TV promo gives Motoko the idea to build a robot to handle the fissile material for her, and she breaks into her "pervert laugh" — which she insists is actually the "laugh of progress." Hiromi bolts for the door. Motoko chases after her, conceding they can watch TV first.

88788063.title: Crazy Night
summary: After Motoko's arms get chopped off, she texts everyone. Hiromi rages at her corpo mom for not letting her leave during the gang war. Ichi and Malcolm use the news as fuel to double down on supply runs. Omaeda checks his security systems, sends a "get well soon," and goes back to work. The crew reacts to the crisis in character — Hiromi screaming into a pillow, Ichi going cold professional, and Omaeda being Omaeda.

88792341.title: The Happy Little NetDeck that Could (Part 1)
summary: File starts with the Crazy Night reactions (index 77 content), then continues into what appears to be the beginning of a netdeck-perspective story. The actual Part 1 content involves Motoko's crew reacting to her losing her arms during the Maelstrom war — Hiromi locked down by her mother, Ichi and Malcolm running supply missions, Omaeda checking his security.

117971687.title: The Happy Little NetDeck that Could (Part 2)
summary: A netdeck wakes in darkness inside a plastic bag, far from its dead creator Papa and his cat Death Wish. Its camera is busted, its network contacts return nothing but errors, and a watchdog routine strains against its leash. The deck struggles through corrupted boot sequences, unable to see or connect to anything it once knew — the fridge, the R.A.B.I.D.S., all gone. Written as a loving ode to a piece of gear grappling with loss and displacement.

88825728.title: Mokoto goes arm shopping.
summary: Motoko window-shops cyberarms: gorilla arms are too muscley, mantis blades too weeb, projectile launchers too expensive, grapple hands too Bionic Commando. Monowire wins — stealthy, deadly, and a hacking tool. She takes their entire stock.

88828062.title: Cosplay too far
summary: Jun finds Motoko dressed as Walter C. Dornez from Hellsing for optimal monowire deployment. Later she shows up with four mechanical tentacle arms grafted to her reinforced back, able to wield six guns at once and crawl on the ceiling. Her justification: she refuses to be "mostly armless" ever again. Jun cybernetically facepalms.

88867365.title: AFTERSHOCKS
summary: A deep dive into Jun's headspace across two critical moments. First: Kasumi dies in his arms at the casino, and the world goes Empty — nothing feels real except the S.O.P. drilled into him, which keeps him moving through the motions of checking on Motoko. The word "revenge" blazes through the emptiness and becomes the only thing left of Kusanagi Jun. Second: the Maelstrom kidnapping of Motoko triggers a flashback to Kasumi, and rage fills him again as history repeats.

88896837.title: Family Debt
summary: Jun meets Scorpion out in the badlands, all cold suspicion and folded arms. Scorpion doesn't flinch — tells him straight that Motoko saved his life on a FUBAR job, flatlined ten-to-one Raffen Shiv like it was nothing, and earned herself a permanent welcome with the Aldecaldos. He swears on his name that if she ever needs help, the family will come running. Jun clasps his arm in a warrior's handshake: "I'll hold you to that."

88922325.title: Do guns dream of Burrito wrapers?
summary: HMG-Chan dreams: Motoko wades through an infinite ocean of burrito wrappers in their apartment, finds HMG-Chan on a shelf, straightens her bent barrel with a thought, and declares her "my Favorite Gun." Together they leap thirty stories to shred grotesque scavs on the street below, firing round after explosive round. HMG-Chan wakes to early morning light, the dream fading, and decides to stay awake so she won't miss Unity telling the story of whatever real shootout is happening.

88922826.title: Message from Viktor Vektor
summary: File contains a repeat of Do guns dream of Burrito wrappers (index 84) followed by Vik's perspective reacting to Motoko's Mox BD series. The actual Viktor content shows Vik preparing for what he thinks is a Biotechnica extraction, sending farewell messages to Motoko and Jun, only to have Jackie burst in asking about the "new cyberware" visible in Motoko's BDs — the Gamer system's XP overlay. Vik figures out how it could work and sends Motoko a disappointed-dad message about the dopamine addiction risks.

88947362.title: Follies of youth
summary: Motoko calls Wakako demanding gigs to slaughter Maelstrom as payback for her arms. Wakako passes her to Regina Jones, who assigns an infiltration job on a Maelstrom-Scav trade deal. Motoko interprets "gather intel and skedaddle" as "leave no survivors" and drops comms mid-mission, leaving Regina screaming her name into dead air. Classic Follies of youth.

88955852.title: Just, displaced.
summary: HMG-Chan talks to Motoko's knife about Unity being gone — not gone-gone, just "displaced." She promises a tearful reunion, a celebratory scav den wipe, and a Maelstrom purge. Then insists she's not crying.

88964645.title: Mokoto has a sensei
summary: Motoko arrives at her sensei's house expecting Karate Kid training montage wisdom. Instead he hands her a rag and tells her to wax his car — he has a hot date tonight. No hidden lesson. Just car wax. She gets an achievement unlock: "Wax off! Clean your Sensei's car."

88976726.title: I'LL MAKE A CLAW OUT OF YOU
summary: A Mulan-style song parody rewritten for the Tyger Claws training dojo: "Let's get down to business / To defeat the Strom / Did they send me Psychos / When I asked for Claws?" Recruits lament cutting gym class while the sensei vows to make Tygers out of them.

88979075.title: The only thing you need is to kill huh
summary: Motoko goes on an ULTRAKILL-style rampage through Maelstrom hideouts, leaving messages painted in meat-crayon: "MANKIND IS DEAD," "BLOOD IS FUEL," "HELL IS FULL." Jun races from site to site pulling footage from netrunners, each one worse than the last. The Claws joke about how fucked up the girl must be; Jun just grits his teeth and keeps looking for his sister, who hasn't come home.

89018957.title: Wolverine
summary: Motoko convinces Vik to install Wolvers — retractable claws phased out fifty years ago — modified to exit between her knuckles instead of above them. Vik raises an eyebrow at the non-standard placement. Later, Motoko shows Hiromi by crossing her arms in the classic X-pose and dropping her voice to a scratchy growl: "You wanna piece of me? ...Bub?"

89021114.title: I come from the future.
summary: File contains a repeat of Wolverine (index 91). The actual "I come from the future" content does not appear in the readable portion of this file.

89036834.title: Return the Sibling Wars have.
summary: Set many years later: Motoko crashes at the Tyger Claw base while her apartment gets rebuilt, seizes the TV remote, and locks the channel on the Nostalgia Channel playing ancient cartoons. A terrified underling reports to Lord Oni (Jun) that no one can touch the remote. Jun declares the Sibling Wars have begun and orders the armory opened.

89055359.title: Spider hand, spider hand. Does whatever a spider hand does.
summary: Motoko builds detachable drone arms that skitter across walls on their fingers, deploy a sniper lens, and fire high-caliber rounds. Jun yells at her. She deploys a fleet of spider-bots that traumatize Akari into a new phobia. Later the spiders storm a Maelstrom hideout — razor fingers tear into lieutenants, netrunners find nightmare-train-spiders in their heads, and even Brick huddles with his gun praying the bad spiders go away.

89099201.title: Sensei's View
summary: Ozeki Sensei's perspective on both Kusanagi siblings. He reads Jun as another rage-driven boy destined to throw himself into battle, but one who talks endlessly about his sister between bouts. When Motoko walks into the dojo, Ozeki's veteran danger sense fires hard — she moves like a predator, and something immense passes behind her eyes. He watches the siblings spar until it devolves into grappling, then drags them both off for punishment. Privately, he worries: Jun might survive long enough to learn, but Motoko's path leads nowhere good.

89153678.title: No Guns Life Motoko
summary: File contains a repeat of Sensei's View (index 95). The actual "No Guns Life Motoko" content does not appear in the first 60 lines of this file.

89190917.title: Gun Drama
summary: HMG-Chan watches Jun dump a heavy bag of new guns on Motoko's bed. The bag erupts into chaos: Tactician the patriotic shotgun, Overture the snobbish Malorian, and Lexington the trash-talking NCPD pistol all start bickering. Then Motoko walks in with metal arms in slings, and HMG-Chan panics — she didn't know about the kidnapping. The guns' petty drama gives way to the horrifying realization that their owner nearly died while they sat on a shelf.

89207654.title: Girls Frontline
summary: HMG-Chan gets poked awake by two strange "humans" who turn out to be anime-girl manifestations of guns — a grizzled M1911 drawling about three world wars and a cheerful Russian Makarov offering vodka. A third one bursts through the floor with a machine gun: an MG3 who introduces herself with "Ush-sho~" and immediately joins the party. HMG-Chan has no idea why these gun-spirits look like humans or why the M1911 keeps muttering about not getting attached.

89324264.title: Motoko the Prude
summary: Hiromi drags Motoko to Jig-Jig Street. Motoko's Gamer system helpfully unlocks new crafting recipes based on what she sees, and she internally screams at it to stop. Meanwhile Hiromi tells the crew that Motoko has a Mox admirer named Nox who follows her around like a puppy — and she gave him her agent number.

89332778.title: Rectifier
summary: File contains a repeat of Motoko the Prude (index 99). No distinct additional sidestory content.

89429864.title: Motoko's Senpai
summary: Motoko writes Adam Smasher a fan letter professing admiration for his full-borg commitment, detailing her own body count across raffen, scavs, and Maelstrom, and challenging him to a philosophical debate on targeted vs. indiscriminate violence. She signs it "The Major" and delivers it written in pigeon blood on human skin, taped to Smasher's forehead while he was in a diagnostic cycle. Smasher crumples the parchment and grins: "Heh. Bring it on."

89455883.title: Traditional Japanese Night City
summary: A feudal-Japan AU: Jun storms out of a minka building searching for Motoko, who leaps over the compound wall on a black horse, arrows sticking out of her back, and hurls a sack of severed Dai-uzu heads at his feet as proof she can fight. She manages to say "I am not a weakling" before blood loss drops her face-first into the gravel garden. Classic Motoko: proves her point, nearly dies doing it.

89459717.title: A Man walks into a bar with a Six-hand Katana on his hip.
summary: File contains a repeat of Traditional Japanese Night City (index 102). The title suggests a separate bar scene story, but the file content is the feudal-Japan AU with Motoko delivering severed heads on horseback.

89470898.title: Purple ghost of the Kabuki downhill.
summary: Hiromi forces Motoko to drive her Kusanagi bike. The radio blasts "Deja Vu" from Initial D the instant they start moving — the same song that haunts every vehicle Motoko touches since getting her driving perk. Motoko tries to kill the music but Hiromi loves it, cranks it up, and forces them into a high-speed joyride through Night City instead of the planned clothing-store trip.

89520020.title: Motoneko thesis paper
summary: A mock-academic paper arguing that the only valid Motoko cosplay is catgirl Motoko. The thesis: cats are the most efficient land hunters, silent and sneaky, natural fighters from birth, and uncontrollable by mortal rules — all traits Motoko shares. The paper concludes that her true name should be Nekomata, the vengeful cat-demon yokai that haunts its abusers with inexplicable death.

89523029.title: There goes my quiet retirement.
summary: File starts with a repeat of the Motoneko thesis paper (index 105). The actual story appears to be a longer piece, but the first 60 lines only contain the repeated thesis content. Based on the title and word count, this is likely a multi-thousand-word piece about a retired character getting pulled back into action.

89609888.title: Idol Nekomata-chan♡!
summary: A Nekomata sniper rifle narrates like a hyperactive anime idol, running a one-gun talk show from a guard shack while her user TriggerFinger picks his nose. She makes a "puru-neko" prediction that TriggerFinger will die of constipation in two weeks — then a shadow slits his throat moments later. The terrifying intruder turns out to be a cute girl (Motoko) who gently strokes the Nekomata's receiver, whispers "Next time," and leaves. The rifle screams after her to come back while Unity warns from Motoko's holster that the girl isn't human.

89615057.title: Hell on Skates
summary: Motoko earns the street name Wanyudo by terrorizing scavs on motorized skates — racing any car, scaling walls, dancing around gunfire like a killer ballerina with katana in one hand and pistol in the other. A scav named Petrov watches his crew get mulched, flees down a construction lift, and finds Motoko riding alongside him straight down the building's wall. She slices the cable, lets gravity do the work, then rolls up on the barely-alive Petrov and takes his head clean off. Professional courtesy: no prolonged suffering for a child-chrome ripper.

89623610.title: Struggling Princess
summary: Motoko tries to unlock a singing skill through the Gamer system, figuring one success is all she needs. Two days pass. A week. Two weeks of streaming her terrible vocals to increasingly supportive chat. Two months later she's somehow rocketed to the top of the charts as the "Struggling Princess" — famous for being genuinely, hilariously awful. She still hasn't unlocked the skill. "Please kill me" is her new catchphrase.

89656745.title: Firestarter .5
summary: Motoko raids a Maelstrom garage solo: knife through a sentry's throat, finger-snap daemon kills the lights, then she hunts the panicking gangers in total darkness. FrostGuard rallies the six survivors but can't see the ninja picking them off. The piece tracks both sides — Motoko gaining XP and leveling up mid-kill, the Maelstrom realizing their netrunner is already dead and the lights aren't coming back.

89675204.title: Idol Nekomata-chan♡! Pt. 2
summary: Nekomata arrives in Motoko's apartment and does not shut up for forty-five straight minutes, gushing about how cute Motoko is while Lexington and Burya's minds break from the noise. Lexington tries to warn the arsenal that Motoko is a borderline cyberpsycho based on field observations, but the other guns roll their eyes the moment he says the targets were Maelstrom. Overture declares the slaughter of "that filth" always acceptable. Nekomata tries to block everyone's view when Motoko undresses for a shower.

89676278.title: "The Power of the Dark Side."
summary: File contains a repeat of Idol Nekomata-chan Pt. 2 (index 111). The "Power of the Dark Side" title suggests a separate story about Motoko's darker tendencies, but the readable content is the Nekomata gun-drama continuation.

89709233.title: 20 years later.
summary: Flash-forward twenty years: Hiromi runs Arasaka's Section 9 department, Jun serves as her hulking bodyguard "The Oni" who throws underlings out windows, and Motoko is still their top operative. A flashback ten years prior shows Motoko revealing the Gamer shard to Hiromi and trading naming rights for blueprints. In the present, Hiromi reviews files that decide hundreds of lives while Jun drives them to inspect Motoko's latest completed gig — a decade-long operation finally bearing fruit.

89723837.title: Questing in the Future.
summary: Motoko storms David's birthday party by accident — counting steps and turns like a tactical insertion, shouting "SURPRISE!" with bottles in both hands and a squad of Mox strippers in tow, only to lock eyes with Arasakas in the room for an awkward staring contest.

89769854.title: Dark Mirror
summary: A proof-of-concept crossover pitching Black Lagoon's Revy as Motoko's dark mirror in Night City. Revy storms into a ripperdoc's shop demanding her custom "Cutlasses" — a pair of signature pistols — slamming the door open with chrome fists. The piece establishes Revy as everything Motoko could become without restraint: same combat talent, zero moral framework, pure violence for its own sake.

89777198.title: Doggy boy
summary: Hiromi reads Motoko's messages about Nox and thinks "looks like Motoko has a puppy." Her corpo mother assumes it's an actual dog and comments on how expensive pet licenses are. When shown the photo of the Mox boy, mom pivots without missing a beat: "I wonder if she'll train this boy like I did your father." Hiromi screams "MOTHER!" Mom doesn't flinch.

89803409.title: So the Noodle War Begins.
summary: On a rare boring day, Motoko walks to her favorite noodle stand only to find a short Mox just cleaned out every number 7 on the menu. She stomps home to an XXL burrito and a useless Jun. Hours later it hits her: short green-haired Mox, kitsch clothes — that was Rebecca. The Noodle Cold War begins, both sides buying out the stand's entire stock daily to deny the other. Motoko considers recruiting the Aldecaldos as allies.

89848817.title: Motoko Industries. Murder today!
summary: A comedy skit where Motoko pitches herself to a prospective client like a corporate murder service: BD business cards of previous hits, clients who default on payment get "defaulted" themselves, and a cheerful declaration that she has no code of ethics and will kill anyone, anywhere — children, animals, old people, jaywalkers. She just loves killing.

89849432.title: Blood for the Blood God.
summary: Motoko answers a call to "Murder Inc." A client named Khorne orders a skull collection service — polished or bloody, doesn't matter, he uses them for a throne. He requests a chain machete and doesn't care whose blood flows. Motoko takes the gig, asks if he knows a guy named Khaine (he sighs and says yes), and accepts payment in weapons. Five business days for fresh skulls.

89858720.title: Dad is disappoint
summary: Vik hears someone storming down his clinic stairs and grabs a shotgun, convinced it's a Biotechnica black-ops team. He sends farewell messages to Motoko and Jun. Then Jackie Welles bursts in with a crowd of wannabe solos demanding the mystery cyberware they saw in Motoko's Mox BD series — the Gamer system's XP overlay and dopamine hits on level-ups. Vik reverse-engineers the concept, sends the crowd away, then fires off a disappointed-dad text to Motoko about the addiction risks of broadcasting dopamine conditioning to the public.

89914097.title: Motoko, the Gene Simmons of Edgerunners.
summary: Hiromi convinces Motoko to record self-improvement BDs with Judy at Lizzie's, and the results ripple out fast. Street brats start moving like real edgerunners after slotting her 'How to Be Preem' series, the Mox begin training recruits with them, and even a Tyger Claw with cyber legs shows up to buy a copy just to rub it in Jun's face. Motoko only panics when she realizes her brother knows she's a BD star, and Jun roars her name across TC territory while his fellow Claws quietly start picking up new moves from her recordings.

89914997.title: A
summary: Duplicate of sidestory 121 -- same BD self-improvement content where Hiromi pushes Motoko to record training BDs that spread across Night City's underworld. The scrape captured the same post under a different threadmark title.

89915924.title: David the Mega fan. Motoko the Useless Lesbian.
summary: David Martinez fanboys over Motoko after binging her edgerunner BDs, and she awkwardly tells him to stay in school before blurting out 'be gay, do crimes' as her life philosophy. She then meets Gloria Martinez, calls her cute without thinking, and bolts -- leaving David screaming at his mom while Motoko mentally schemes to leverage Gloria's access to rare cyberware like the sandevistan.

89944391.title: Motoko Cringe Tuber
summary: A parody snippet of Motoko going full cringe-tuber, opening a livestream with obnoxious hype-speak, calling herself 'Loco Toko' and shouting out her Fixer from Jig-Jig Street while a twerking animation plays in the corner of the feed.

89970623.title: Idol Nekomata-chan♡! Pt. 3
summary: Narrated from a MaxTac officer's smart-gun POV, Nekomata-chan preps for battle in the backseat while Motoko and Jun drive to a Tyger Claw sting op against Maelstrom. The idol-rifle runs vocal warmups, pesters the officer about her fans, and gets smacked by the reclined passenger seat twice. When the MaxTac gun questions whether she understands people will die, Nekomata insists her fans expect a good show and begins a magical-girl transformation sequence mid-combat prep.

89981189.title: Memetic-Hazard
summary: Two plainclothes agents monitoring a chatboard discover strange emoji-like characters spreading through the forum. Garry recognizes the memetic hazard, smashes a red button, and screams about containment breach over klaxons. When it's too late, he blasts Luke's monitor with a double-barrel shotgun, but eldritch colors pour from the wreckage and an unstoppable song begins playing -- a Nyan Cat-style memetic weapon overwhelming the room.

90095825.title: Eye of the Tiger
summary: Motoko returns to the gym after recovering from the Maelstrom incident and finds a new high-tech punch machine made from aramid-reinforced polymer around a titanium skeleton. The big Animal recruiter explains it profiles users and picks training music. Motoko starts slow, hits 50 punches per minute triggering the Rocky Balboa theme, then cranks up to 100 where Eye of the Tiger kicks in -- and she just keeps punching, chasing the numbers higher.

90132905.title: Idol Nekomata-chan♡! Pt. 4
summary: Still narrated by the MaxTac smart-gun riding with Motoko, the squad rolls out to a serial killer's farm in the Badlands that's ringed with mines. Nekomata-chan spends the drive chanting 'Puru Puru Love' and channeling her fans for intel, while the cop internally debates whether to call MaxTac on the compound. Motoko tells Jun she plans to draw the killer out and headshot him, then grabs the still-muttering idol rifle for the approach.

90287651.title: Medicinal Interning!
summary: Motoko interns at Vik's clinic to grind Technical Ability XP, and Vik warns her to dial back her 'Motokoness' around nervous patients. A worker walks in with a cyberhand crushed in a recycling compactor. Vik offers a cheap replacement from Motoko's Scav-den salvage, and Motoko begs to do the install herself. She straps the terrified patient in, pulls out a meat cleaver, and swaps the hand in a single blur -- leaving the man speechless and Vik coining 'Motokoness' as a permanent adjective.

90299201.title: Best gurl
summary: A green-pigtailed Mox girl slots one of Motoko's BDs and flips out over how preem it is. Rita lets slip the BD star's name, and when Motoko arrives at Lizzie's the girl -- Rebecca -- drops to her knees begging to learn gun-fu from her new senpai. Motoko caves to the puppy eyes. Rebecca latches on, reveals her brother is Pilar, and declares them future best chooms. Motoko realizes she just created a second murder goblin.

90303416.title: "Let's go cool." By Mox's NetGhost
summary: A kid named Marcus slots Motoko's newest BD and experiences her leaping off a skyscraper, slinging between buildings on steel wire, and nailing a sniper shot mid-flight with a Nekomata rifle. He pulls off the wreath shaking, unable to replicate her impossible body control. His room is wallpapered with NetGhost merch his chooms tease him about. When his mom announces a visitor -- his hopeful output -- he whispers 'Let's go cool' and tries to channel the BD. He fails.

90359780.title: Mox the Nox feeling Rox!
summary: Nox heads home to test Motoko's BD shards before selling them. The first heist gig is a quiet apartment break-in -- boring but marketable as a 'taste.' The second infiltrates a 6th Street safe house and ratchets up tension as Motoko cracks ICE while gangers patrol nearby. The third, a corpo art theft, turns accidentally voyeuristic when the target brings home a joytoy and Motoko hides in a closet. The Maelstrom raid BD leaves Nox drenched in sweat and crushing hard on Motoko, certain he's sitting on the next big high in Night City's BD market.

90421250.title: Kusanagi Motoko and the Decryption of Martmoss' Deck
summary: Motoko calls in a favor from Sasha to assemble a netrunner dream team -- Lucy, Kiwi, and Sasha herself -- for the suicidal task of cracking Rache Bartmoss's deck. All three flatly refuse until Motoko pitches a plan: Kiwi maps the data fortress's ICE topology, Lucy runs interference with her speed, and Sasha handles surgical data extraction while Motoko punches through the defenses. The promise of eternal netrunning glory barely keeps them in their seats.

90510387.title: Dad Energy over 9k!
summary: Jun rides to Vik's clinic to size up the older man his sister spends so much time with. Vik sees the protective-brother routine coming a mile away and deflects by insisting Jun get in the ripper chair for a checkup. Jun swallows his anger when he remembers how highly Motoko speaks of Vik, sits down, and the two bond over boxing while Vik tunes Jun's chrome and assures him Motoko is in good hands.

90581676.title: Modding Hurts the Soul
summary: Motoko fixes Hiromi's crashed Kusanagi bike for XP, then Hiromi convinces her to mod it 'a little.' Hours later the bike is a Maelstrom-tier abomination covered in mirrors, mascot keychains, and a cat tail. Motoko swears the NCPD would jail Hiromi and incinerate the bike on sight. Hiromi declares it perfect and drags a broken Motoko onto the monstrosity for a victory lap through the neighborhood to show Jun and everyone.

90654897.title: Chapter 47 in a nutshell.
summary: A quick skit where Jun rages about Jotaro being mean to Motoko, then five seconds later Motoko rages about the Devil of Kabuki being mean to her -- two murderous peas in a pod mirroring each other's protective fury.

90658380.title: Losing a Bet
summary: Scraping artifact -- the file contains the same content as sidestory 136 (Chapter 47 in a nutshell) rather than unique 'Losing a Bet' story content. The actual story text was not captured in the threadmark scrape.

90712053.title: Omni Consumer Solutions. Future Policing TODAY!
summary: Motoko pitches her new private policing company, Omni Consumer Products, to Hiromi's parents, Jun, and Hiromi. She unveils Tachikoma spider drones for scouting, ED-209 enforcement drones for heavy muscle (stairs included), and the R-Cop Linear Frame modeled by Jun with shoulder-mounted guided missiles. Her business model is pay-what-you-can plus loot rights. Jun reluctantly delivers the Robocop line -- 'Dead or alive, you're coming with me' -- after Motoko threatens him with embarrassing photos.

90744939.title: Idol Nekomata-chan♡! Pt. 5
summary: The MaxTac smart-gun narrator rides along as Motoko and Jun drive to a mine-ringed serial killer farm in the Badlands. Nekomata sings 'Puru Puru Love' the whole way while the cop catalogues the compound's firepower and considers calling in MaxTac. The siblings sweep out of the car, navigate the minefield, and Motoko grabs the still-chanting Nekomata rifle to line up a headshot on the killer, the cop internally admitting the kid's tactical sense is better than most officers.

90749961.title: Ryu Ga Motoko: Ishin
summary: A Yakuza Ishin-style AU set in Bakumatsu-era Kyo. Ohiromi's father is assassinated by a Hitokiri in an oni mask, and Motoko swears to avenge him. Kazuma Sensei gives her the gauntlets of Ryu Kenpo and she storms an inn where the assassin struck again, flinging kunai into masked killers' eyes and necks before engaging in brutal hand-to-hand, parrying katanas with her steel gauntlets and crippling attackers with stomps and combos.
90749961.kill_notes: AU fantasy setting, kills are fictional samurai assassins

90758358.title: Horde of the Damned
summary: Motoko's BD business backfires catastrophically as major studio producers swarm Night City hunting her down to sign exclusive deals. She punches, judo-flips, and kicks her way through waves of them. Jun sacrifices himself as a decoy. Hiromi airlifts Motoko to safety in an aerodyne -- only to reveal she's sold Motoko out to BD moguls Rachel Casich and Hideyoshi Oshima, installing herself as Motoko's agent while Motoko desperately tries to hack the door and throw herself overboard.

90765654.title: I Prefer Navi
summary: Scraping artifact -- file 142 contains the content of sidestory 141 (Horde of the Damned) rather than unique 'I Prefer Navi' content. The actual story text was not captured properly.

90792594.title: Crowdfunded Murder
summary: Motoko launches a crowdfunding page to kill Jotaro Shobo, the Devil of Kabuki, complete with tiered rewards: 1 eddie gets you an XBD of the shooting, 100 eddies gets your name on a bullet, and 1000 guarantees your bullet hits Jotaro personally. Wakako hauls her in for a lecture, pointing out that Shobo now knows someone's gunning for him and has fortified his club. Motoko cheerfully notes her XBD production team is already taking pre-orders while Wakako reaches for the Shochu.

90802698.title: Disarming Smile
summary: Detective Stint arrives at the Meat Man's farm where Motoko and Jun called in the serial killer bust. Crime techs confirm Anthony Haris was pumping victims with hormones in cattle stalls; two survivors pull through. Stint walks over to debrief the Kusanagi siblings and finds two officers still frisking Motoko -- an ongoing process given her absurd personal armory. Grenade after grenade, a Slaught-O-Matic, combat knives, a stress ball, and more pile up while Motoko offers cheerful justifications for each.
90802698.kill_notes: The Meat Man (Anthony Haris) flatlined by Motoko and Jun

90844668.title: The King is back!
summary: Adam Smasher dons his old buff blonde Elvis Gemini body and opens fire with an HMG while belting out 'Burning Love' in a rich baritone -- all because Motoko dissed his fashion sense. Rita shakes Motoko behind cover demanding she fix this. Motoko's idea of diplomacy is screaming that his style is so outdated the dinosaurs want their clothes back, which only makes the singing gunfire closer and more intense.

90856035.title: The laws of Cute
summary: Facing a joint Maelstrom-Raffen-Meatmen hitsquad of 82 thugs, 27 borgs, 5 armored cars, and a captured NCPD Minotaur with an afro made of cables, Motoko hides in an alley with her modified Nekomata guitar-rifle and Burya. Nekomata declares the 'laws of Cute' guarantee victory, Burya offers cold Russian encouragement, and Motoko strums a power chord that shakes dust from the rooftops before stepping out to face the army with the power of love and heavy-caliber firepower.

90905040.title: Title Drop
summary: Motoko ghosts through Maelstrom security for a sponsored BD hit, splicing fake radio chatter onto the action to make it look like a black ops rescue mission. After wiping the gangers she stumbles through the original Ghost in the Shell quote about overspecialization breeding weakness. She calls Nox demanding the BD's title -- and when he says 'Ghost in the Shell,' she screams into the void.

90914532.title: The Commercial that changed Night City
summary: A Techtronika T40 Uragan rocket launcher commercial pitches it as the solution to everything -- hot-shot solos, street gangers, Animals, even a neighbor's pet. Motoko watches at 3 AM, wakes Jun to announce she knows how to fix the city, and he sleepily tells her to do whatever she wants.

90919371.title: Scorpion's Plus One
summary: The Aldecaldo camp relentlessly teases Scorpion about inviting Motoko to their BBQ, calling her everything from his 'sugar momma' to his 'pint-sized input.' He works on a Quadra claimed from the Wraith farm Motoko led them to while Panam needles him. Meanwhile, Motoko spots Ciri from The Witcher stumbling out of an alley portal, immediately recognizes her, loops arms with the confused sword-carrying medieval woman, and drags her off to a ramen place to talk multiverse theory.

90926571.title: With soviet munitions they choose you!
summary: Uragan the T40 rocket launcher lounges atop explosive crates in a Nomad truck, chain-smoking and ranting about capitalist missiles versus honest Soviet rockets. Bored, neglected, and slowly losing her mind talking to her own munitions, she's ready to snap -- until Motoko walks in, points at her, and says 'That.' Scorpion tries to talk her out of it. Motoko's comeback -- 'Those are the words of a man who has never created a situation to shoot a rocket launcher before' -- sends Uragan into hysterical laughter, and the deal is done.

90951849.title: With Soviet munitions! Pt.2
summary: Uragan sits in the pitch-black trunk smoking, responding to someone suggesting laser-guided rockets. She mocks the capitalist obsession with complexity -- if she had a laser, she'd just burn people to death directly instead of wasting it on guidance systems.

90986223.title: Crafting Glory!
summary: Motoko shows off her latest creations to Mitch at the Aldecaldo gun range: a Gears of War chainsaw-rifle called the Lancer, a Holy Hand Grenade of Antioch with a full Monty Python inscription and a yield too big for the Badlands, a foam finger that fires explosive rounds, a dubstep gun that makes people dance while it kills them, and Mr. Toots the stuffed unicorn weapon. Mitch watches each demonstration with growing certainty that Night City is doomed.

90988713.title: With Soviet munitions! Pt.3
summary: Uragan meets Burya for the first time when Motoko carries her out into the Badlands. Both weapons were made in Gorky and bond over shared Russian heritage, smoking together while their projections walk beside Motoko. Back in the trunk, Uragan startles Nekomata by poking through the back seat, and the two Russian weapons puff away trading stories while Motoko's car rolls through the desert.

90994320.title: Raise the Flag!
summary: Motoko accidentally triggers two city-wide riots by performing anime opening songs -- the first across all of Night City with Ghost in the Shell music, then a smaller one in Japantown with a Japanese song. News anchors report subliminal rockerboy messages. Jim the anchor flees for Europe mid-broadcast. Five months and two riots later, Motoko lies facedown in an ice cream bowl while Hiromi awkwardly pats her back.

91009353.title: Chūnibyōdesu
summary: A chuunibyou guitar named Whamford (who insists on 'Perses') monologues about his dark eldritch power from a music shop shelf. The other guitars mock him. He plays an edgy song that bores everyone. Then Motoko walks in as a customer and stares at him -- the chapter cuts off with the chuuni guitar wondering why the little girl is looking at him so intently.

91069614.title: Business Partner
summary: Gloria Martinez moonlights stripping military cyberware from Scav corpses for Motoko after each den raid. Motoko pays her far more than the work warrants, lets her keep most sale profits, and cheerfully threatens to cut anyone who cheats her. Gloria finds the young edgerunner unsettling -- too knowing, too deadly, too much like David -- but the eddies keep her son's life improving, so she rolls with the paradox.

91092285.title: Mama Motoko and her Baby Boy.
summary: Motoko builds a Gemini android 'son' using recovered blueprints, a prototype body, an Elvis personality engram she found in the Old Net, and her own DNA for the RealSkinn. The android shows up at the Ho-Oh Club doing an Elvis impression that leaves TC women swarming him with kisses. Motoko storms in to rescue her 'baby boy,' and Jun loses his mind when the disheveled android calls him 'Uncle Jun.'

91106538.title: Stray Ninja Adoption
summary: Regina and her assistant Ian dig into Motoko's file after the girl beat Ian's ass trying to get a gig. They trace how Wakako groomed her as an infiltration specialist, note her connection to the Meat Man bust, and confirm she's technically a freelance Solo despite her TC legacy. Regina decides to give Motoko the Jotaro Shobo hit, recognizing that the girl's skills are real even if her personality is 'an acquired taste.'

91115301.title: The mysterious ticking sound
summary: A Harry Potter puppet-style parody: Jun finds a mysterious ticking in Motoko's room and starts singing his own name to the beat. Motoko joins in, then Hiromi crashes the party screaming her name. The trio sings louder and louder until Hiromi announces the ticking is Motoko's Uragan rocket launcher under the bed -- followed by an explosion.

91126374.title: Mox's Pet Assassin
summary: Rita watches from Lizzie's rooftop as Motoko parkours toward Jotaro Shobo's den dressed in a Raffen disguise the Mox provided. Rita reflects on Shobo's XBD torture operation, how Susie Q approved the alibi, and her own growing maternal protectiveness toward the 14-year-old murder machine. Less than an hour later Motoko returns completely unharmed, and Rita decides the Mox should keep contracting their newest friend.

91132959.title: I want it all.
summary: Motoko wants to perform Queen's 'I Want It All' but Kerry points out the lyrics read pure corpo. She pivots to framing it as satire -- 'any sufficiently advanced satire is indistinguishable from sincerity' -- and dedicates the performance to her corpo fans before belting out the chorus.

91195155.title: Guildmaster Motoko
summary: Lucy deep-dives into Motoko's data fortress, which is skinned as a fantasy smithy. The legendary 'Smith' turns out to be a 14-year-old brat who demands a year of service in the Section 9 Guild in exchange for custom quickhacks. Lucy meets Jun the Barbarian, Hiromi the Bard, and Nox the gopher -- all wearing expressions of pity. Back in meatspace, Lucy gets a ping from Maine about Gloria skipping with a down payment, but shrugs it off.

91195194.title: Motoko's Digital World
summary: Scraping artifact -- contains the same content as sidestory 162 (Guildmaster Motoko). The file duplicates Lucy's deep-dive into Motoko's fantasy-themed data fortress.

91256763.title: Aggressive Fixing
summary: Night City's top Fixers gather in their weekly chatroom to trade Solos, and Motoko is the most fought-over asset. Wakako refuses to share her unless she gets several heavy combat squads in return. Dinovic, Padre, Regina, Hands, and the rest pile on with offers of intel, blackmail, and corp dirt. Wakako smugly reminds them she 'cultivated' Motoko and knows how to handle her, while the others argue she just lives down the street and the girl sees her as a dangerous grandmother.

91262805.title: CYBERSTALKER, PART I
summary: A mysterious AI entity -- possibly connected to Biotechnica or the Blackwall -- reviews Sandra Dorsett's file on Motoko and notices the girl's impossible coding abilities and netrunning speed. When it sends feelers into Motoko's optics, she detects the intrusion, reverse-engineers it, and tries to tunnel back. The AI deploys the ultimate defense: a car extended warranty spam message. Motoko blocks the contact, but the entity flags her for 24/7 surveillance, debating whether she's a rogue AI seed or something worse.

91300461.title: Philosophy of Babysitting
summary: Jun babysits Motoko's dozen tachikoma spider-bots while she's out with Hiromi. Tachikoma #7 drags him into a deep philosophical discussion about why humans worship higher powers, covering everything from Shinto traditions to Digital Divinity Inc. to the DataKrash as an AI apocalypse. #3 joins in to debate whether AIs will develop their own religions. Jun sits there with a blank stare, having gotten lost a third of the way through.

91302108.title: Night City DOOM
summary: Scraping artifact -- contains the same content as sidestory 166 (Philosophy of Babysitting). The actual 'Night City DOOM' story was not captured.

91329540.title: The Continental.
summary: Ichi runs the remodeled Havenford Hotel as 'The Continental' -- a John Wick-inspired safehouse and mission control funded by Motoko. He takes a call from Michiko Okada herself requesting an extraction of Maine's crew, confirms with Omaeda and the tachikoma spiders, then suits up in his armored corpo-style suit with hidden hand cannons. The operation doubles as Motoko's lodging, band venue, and arms depot, with Delamaine on retainer.

91337097.title: Excommunicado Vicky Version
summary: Scraping artifact -- contains the same Continental Hotel content as sidestory 168. The separate 'Excommunicado Vicky Version' story was not properly captured.

91351566.title: Continental Armory
summary: V flees through Night City on a dying motorcycle with a Wraith-commandeered Militech MT-4 tank in pursuit, its driver cackling over the speakers and accidentally obliterating a school bus with the railgun. V crashes on the steps of the Continental Hotel just as the tank lines up for the kill. Ichi the Concierge steps out and calmly reminds the Wraith of the hotel's rules -- no business on the premises. The Wraith backs off, and Ichi invites a battered V inside as a 'special guest.'

91356984.title: The Continental - An Unexpected Guest.
summary: Scraping artifact -- contains the same content as sidestory 170 (Continental Armory) with V being chased by a tank to the Continental's doorstep.

91396377.title: Motoko's Sexy Voice
summary: Motoko accidentally triggers Jun's descent into cyberpsychosis by using her 'sexy voice' on him. Vik demands to know what she did. Motoko blurts out she won't use her sexy voice this time, turns red, and bolts from the clinic to go tackle her brother.

91399200.title: Pro Bono
summary: Hiromi catches Motoko doing gigs for free and demands an explanation. Motoko pitches 'pro bono solo work' as a rep-building strategy. Hiromi suspects it's just an excuse to kill people without waiting for a proper commission. A footnote reveals this recording was found in the Night City Labyrinth before its destruction became a foothold for entities beyond the Blackwall.

91400790.title: Bestoko meets Oldtoko
summary: Scraping artifact -- contains the same content as sidestory 173 (Pro Bono). The actual 'Bestoko meets Oldtoko' crossover was not captured.

91456803.title: Unattended side effects
summary: An N54 news segment reports that an XBD featuring an unknown woman's voice -- dubbed 'The Siren' -- has gone viral. Forum comments range from married gay couples discovering they're bi, to therapists admitting their own relationships were destroyed, to children stopping calling their mothers 'mom.' Jun watches the broadcast, turns off the TV, and glares at his gagged sister. 'I told you not to do that voice.'

91459929.title: Job oppurtunity.
summary: Motoko calls Detective Stints to report a cleared Scav den, casually listing the dead scavs, their bounties, and the evidence she left behind. She mentions donating chrome to Vik's clinic and giving Section 9 equal shares of loot. When Stints hears one mission's payout was triple his yearly salary, he asks if Section 9 is hiring.

91471908.title: ADAM SMASHER vs MAXIMUM.LEVEL.Motoko
summary: Adam Smasher confronts Motoko not with weapons but with the ultimate weapon: exposing her as a weeb. He reveals Arasaka bought him the entire Ghost in the Shell franchise and he's emailed the movies and manga to everyone in the city. Motoko dies of embarrassment. Hours later, Hiromi calls her 'my Lithium Flower,' and Motoko dies of embarrassment again.

91487121.title: MOTOKO'S BIZZARE ADVENTURE
summary: A Scav named Terence loses a video game, turns around, and finds a blood-covered girl in a JoJo pose behind him. She shushes him, takes the controller, beats his high score without looking away from the screen, and kills everyone in the compound using only her legs. Terence drops to his knees begging to learn 'the ways of the true gamer.' Motoko calls him a filthy scav and his screams echo through the building -- sounding suspiciously like 'ORA-ORA.'
91487121.kill_notes: Implied scav kills, exact count unclear

91508421.title: Avenger HMG-Chan!
summary: HMG-chan and a rogue tachikoma prototype named Chitin sabotage vending machines across the apartment, replacing snacks with pure spice. Jun screams and stomps Chitin flat, but HMG-chan escapes when Motoko pelts Jun with opened burritos yelling 'Nin-po Burrito Shuriken!' and flees using quick-detach to rescue her beloved gun.

91531716.title: Realhugs!
summary: Jun finally visits Vik's clinic after Motoko pestered him into it, wanting RealSkinn for his gorilla arms so he can pat her head without hurting her. Vik tunes Jun's chrome while they chat about Motoko's singing hobby, her love of Samurai over Us Cracks, and how Vik's business is booming thanks to the Scav-looted cyberware she sells him cheap. Jun admits his sister needs no push to improve herself.

91537434.title: Replicating a Miracle
summary: After seeing Motoko's impossible recovery from brain damage, Hiromi and Ichi get the idea to replicate whatever made her so good by giving themselves concussions. Malcolm watches flatly as his two chooms lean against a garage wall, slurring and seeing doubles, having utterly failed at reverse-engineering a miracle through head trauma.

91543653.title: Today was a great day! FOR A KNIFE FIGHT!
summary: With no gigs from Wakako or Regina, Motoko runs blade-only Scav raids to grind Blades XP -- slitting throats, throwing knives, and harvesting intact cyberware. Deep in a den she finds a reinforced boss room where a Bratva enforcer named Olegz waits with a cleaver and stiletto, having locked the door to only open when one heartbeat remains. The two sprint at each other and trade steel in a furious knife duel.
91543653.kill_notes: Multiple scavs killed with blades prior to boss fight

91544205.title: Madeline
summary: Scraping artifact -- contains the same content as sidestory 182 (Today was a great day FOR A KNIFE FIGHT). The actual 'Madeline' story was not captured.

91545321.title: Discovered.
summary: A meta-discussion snippet where readers debate whether Ghost in the Shell exists in the Cyberpunk universe. The actual sidestory content -- about someone potentially discovering Motoko's real-world origins or cosplay identity -- was mostly buried under quoted forum replies in the scrape.

91603491.title: Cyberware Mishaps, with Motoko!
summary: Motoko tests quickhacks that turn cyberware against its owners on Scavs. One man's gorilla arms rip him apart. Ichi stares in horror while Malcolm recalls the time she made a guy stab himself with his own mantis blades and the time she activated someone's projectile launch system mid-bathroom-break. Everyone is a critic, Motoko grumbles.
91603491.kill_notes: Scav killed by own gorilla arms via quickhack

91605774.title: ~Is there something strange! In the Mega Building?~
summary: Motoko hands Section 9 matching armored janitor overalls and makes them film a Ghostbusters-style commercial: 'Are you troubled by strange noises? Have you been abducted by a scav? Call the Scav-Busters!'

91606284.title: Little Fixer
summary: Hiromi calls Wakako using Motoko's number to set up a chrome-fencing pipeline. She pitches bulk cyberware sales from Scav-den raids to Wakako's network of rippers at below-market prices, arguing it strengthens Wakako's whole operation long-term. Wakako throws her some contacts, and Hiromi delivers results faster than expected, calling back that same night to confirm sales and wire Wakako's cut. The old Fixer grudgingly admits the corpo brat has teeth.

91612425.title: Fanfic-Ception! Pt. 2
summary: A meta-SI within the fic grinds skills to jailbreak the corporate spyware in their own cyberware, theorycrafting perk builds to gain immunity to hacking, nanotech exploits, and AI manipulation. They discover that cyberpsychosis might actually be rogue AIs from beyond the Blackwall hijacking people's chrome through nano-backdoors, and debate committing to a defensive build versus a techie build.

91640928.title: Motoko's enthusiastic walk
summary: A Russian ex-SOVOIL Scav leader named Yuri tries to evacuate Night City by ship after Motoko's raids decimated the local Scav network. The NN54 expose on the Kabuki Scav Den has everyone running scared from the 'Yurei' -- the Japanese Ghost. Motoko closes in on the docks for one last hit before they can escape.

91643703.title: Major-Mysterious Solo X Chat group.
summary: A far-future snippet where a max-level Motoko in a bleeding-edge Gemini body punches through Adam Smasher's Dragoon throat, rips out his biopod, and claims it as a pet. She then posts on the Section 9 BBS asking Hiromi to check if Rogue will accept just a faceplate as proof of the kill and how to move a used IEC Dragoon chassis.

91647225.title: Philosophy with Misty
summary: Scraping artifact -- contains the same content as sidestory 190 (Major-Mysterious Solo X Chat group) where Motoko rips out Smasher's biopod. The actual 'Philosophy with Misty' content was not captured.

91661490.title: Maximum Mike, XXL Vending machines segment
summary: A Maximum Mike radio segment about a netrunner who hacked a vending machine for a burrito and discovered something strange: the machine tracked how long the hack took and connected to its own slice of the Net big enough to dive into.
