Mike speculates it could be corpo surveillance of netrunners, a runner tracking network, or even a Blackwall AI playing games. The netrunner never hacked another vending machine after that.

91662342.title: Professor Motoko X!
summary: Scraping artifact -- contains the same content as sidestory 192 (Maximum Mike vending machine segment). The actual 'Professor Motoko X' content was not captured.

91679727.title: TC Tikes
summary: Tyger Claw boss Saito reviews the aftermath of an assassination attempt at a party where Jun and Motoko saved Okina-san from a Maelstrom-linked hitter named 'Truck.' The siblings took down the borged-out assassin bare-handed, with Jun ripping off his head. Saito considers rewarding Jun and extending an official TC invitation to Motoko, but notes she seems more interested in solo work than gang membership.

91681602.title: GMing a game with Magic Sushi
summary: Section 9 plays a tabletop RPG session with Motoko as GM, referencing magic sushi and typical TTRPG chaos. The crew's in-game antics mirror their real-world dynamic -- Hiromi min-maxes, the boys get into trouble, and Motoko improvises wildly.

91682844.title: Mokoto's School Trip
summary: Motoko tags along on some kind of school trip, her edgerunner instincts clashing with the mundane civilian setting as she treats every shadow like a potential ambush.

91694442.title: Night City Cosplay
summary: Night City residents start cosplaying as Motoko and other edgerunners, treating her growing legend like celebrity fandom. The line between real edgerunner and wannabe blurs as fans adopt her look and style.

91713150.title: Borg Hunter Jun
summary: Jun takes on borged-out targets as a Kamikaze operative, his reputation as the Oni growing. The story follows his perspective as he hunts dangerous cyberpsychos, drawing on the combat skills that make him one of the TC's most effective enforcers.

91745559.title: Sexy Motoko voice overload
summary: Motoko's mature 'sexy voice' continues to cause havoc across Night City. The voice becomes a running problem as more people encounter it and react with everything from infatuation to existential crisis, forcing Jun and others to deal with the fallout.

91746879.title: Humanoid Typhoon
summary: Motoko earns the nickname 'Humanoid Typhoon' for the sheer trail of destruction she leaves behind on gigs. The collateral damage radius around her operations grows large enough that people start comparing her to a natural disaster.

91749489.title: Montage Time!
summary: A training montage as Section 9 levels up together -- running gigs, practicing at the range, upgrading chrome, and building team cohesion under Motoko's chaotic leadership style.

91760334.title: Jackie is introduced to Motoko's Rep.
summary: Jackie Welles gets the rundown on Motoko's street rep and reacts with a mix of disbelief and respect. Whoever briefs him lays out the highlights -- Scav hunter, BD star, Tyger Claw-adjacent ninja, and growing legend -- leaving Jackie wondering what kind of monster girl has been operating in his backyard.

91761783.title: Jackie is introduced to Motoko's Rep. Oxymoron interrupt
summary: A brief comedic follow-up where someone interrupts Jackie's briefing on Motoko's rep to point out an oxymoron in how she's described -- a teenage ninja murder-girl who also genuinely helps people.

91763724.title: The New Hire
summary: Section 9 brings on a new recruit who gets the full orientation experience -- meeting Motoko, seeing the tachikomas, and realizing that working for a teenage edgerunner savant is going to be nothing like a normal gig.

91764735.title: *Morgan Blackhand at home.*
summary: A brief joke snippet imagining the legendary solo Morgan Blackhand at home, reacting to news about Motoko's latest antics in Night City.

91772034.title: *in front of Afterlife*
summary: Motoko shows up at the Afterlife bar, Night City's most exclusive merc hangout. The bouncers, regulars, and Rogue herself have to deal with the chaos that follows whenever the teenage ninja-solo-rockerboy walks through the door.

91790055.title: THREE LEGS!
summary: A short joke about someone discovering -- or imagining -- that Motoko has three legs, playing on the absurdity of her ever-expanding arsenal of chrome and hidden weapons.

91795350.title: Motoko entered the chat
summary: Motoko crashes an online chat or forum, and her presence immediately derails whatever normal conversation was happening as people freak out that the infamous edgerunner is in their channel.

91798014.title: Motoko has an idea
summary: Motoko gets one of her trademark ideas -- the kind that makes everyone around her nervous. The crew braces for impact as she lays out a plan that is equal parts brilliant and terrifying.

91814088.title: The quiet ones.
summary: A look at the quieter members of Night City's underworld -- the fixers, rippers, and support players who keep the machine running behind the scenes while edgerunners like Motoko grab all the headlines.

91823784.title: OOOH YEAH!
summary: Regina sends Motoko after a ripper hiding in a bar. Motoko hacks the cameras, spots the target hitting on the bartender, and borrows Scorpion's brutalized off-road vehicle. She crashes through the bar wall Kool-Aid Man style to 'go through' the ripper as the gig brief instructed, taking the phrase extremely literally.

91825803.title: Regina POV
summary: Regina wrestles with the moral weight of calling a 14-year-old girl to pull off a Kang Tao hit. She lights a cigarette by her window, stares at corpo ads scrolling across the skyline, and dials the number -- feeling like just another cog in the machine she once fought against. Motoko picks up snarky, and Regina sighs.

91826529.title: Motoko talking about the gig
summary: Scraping artifact -- contains the same content as sidestory 212 (Regina POV). The actual 'Motoko talking about the gig' content was not captured.

91838886.title: Motoko Deals!
summary: Motoko delivers her personal rate card to Wakako on a shard. Discounts range from 10% off for prank assistance to 100% off for one honest hug and 'atta girl.' Wakako stares blankly, Motoko announces she'll get that hug someday loud enough for the whole Pachinko Parlor to hear, and Wakako chants 'she's one of my best agents' to keep from putting a hit out on the girl.

91872723.title: Live and Direct @ The Tab
summary: Veteran screamsheet reporter Angie Wu of The Tab follows a lead about a spooked NCPD officer in the Badlands, driving out with her camera guy Sean. She passes a slow-moving armored Quadra on the way. The investigation ties into the Meat Man story Motoko cracked, as Angie digs into the crime scene and the anonymous vigilantes who solved the case the NCPD couldn't.

91873584.title: Cringe BD.
summary: Scraping artifact -- contains the same content as sidestory 215 (Live and Direct @ The Tab). The actual 'Cringe BD' content was not captured.

91944342.title: N54 News on Pon Pon Shit.
summary: N54 anchor Gillean Jordan reports that Us Cracks' 'Pon Pon Shit' is being linked to a spike in cyberpsychosis cases across Night City. The NCPD urges citizens to stop listening. Rumors swirl that the idol group may have released the song intentionally to cause chaos.

91947471.title: Press Release response by Us Cracks
summary: An in-universe media response to the N54 report on Pon Pon Shit, written from the perspective of someone who feels compelled to reply to the news segment. The press release format plays on the absurdity of an idol group being blamed for a citywide cyberpsychosis epidemic.

91973808.title: Rebuild, Repair, Restore. HMG-Chan.
summary: HMG-chan undergoes a major rebuild after taking damage in the Badlands. Motoko painstakingly repairs and upgrades her beloved Militech Mk. 31 heavy machine gun, treating the process like surgery on a family member.

91975059.title: Rebuild, Repair, Restore. HMG-Chan. Interrupt
summary: Motoko slots the last component into HMG-chan and a new personality manifests -- a mobster-talking tough guy in a black overcoat who wants to 'ice' people and declares war on cars. The rebuilt gun immediately starts ordering Motoko around, and she obeys without question.

91977567.title: Proud Corpo Momma
summary: Hiromi's Arasaka corpo parents brag at a mixer about their daughter running a street gang, using a patsy to sell XBDs at the Academy, and having no obvious ties to the operation. Mid-brag, Hiromi's mom takes a call to arrange surplus rocket launchers and armored car deliveries for her daughter's next operation, then returns to the conversation praising little Hiromi's 'enterprising' nature.

91986618.title: Digital Friendship!
summary: The local Net starts changing as AI daemons shaped like Digimon appear -- cleaning up viruses, scrubbing junk data, and being aggressively friendly to netrunners. Netwatch tries to contain them but gets wrecked by fireballs and lightning. The 'Digital World' keeps expanding despite corpo efforts to lock it down. The source? Motoko, who is back in her server scolding Agumon about his diet and pulling Guilmon out of holes he dug under the firewall fence.

91992987.title: Hiromi's advice
summary: Hiromi tells Motoko to stop overthinking a gig. Her plan: Motoko infiltrates a gang by pretending to be a timid recruit armed only with 'these arms and a pencil,' does a Joker-style disappearing pencil trick on the boss, and declares herself the new leader. The guns come down, greedy faces come out, and Section 9 gains a new branch.

91995216.title: Sadist mode Skippy.
summary: Scraping artifact -- contains the same content as sidestory 223 (Hiromi's advice) with the pencil trick gang takeover. The actual 'Sadist mode Skippy' content was not captured.

92046390.title: Mom jumps to conclusions
summary: Hiromi, who knows she has a crush on Motoko, asks her corpo mom for a braindance recorder to 'assess' her friend's abilities. Her mother agrees suspiciously fast, schedules an Arasaka ripper within 90 minutes, and hands Hiromi military-grade encryption for the recordings -- explaining it's standard company policy for employees' sex tapes. Hiromi screams that she's not making a sex tape.

92065113.title: Full of... Lead.
summary: Vik patches up Motoko's dented arms and prescribes laxatives to pass the dozens of bullets still lodged in her torso after she healed by sleeping. Motoko asks about magnetic plungers so Jun doesn't find out the hard way.

92075862.title: The Talk
summary: Motoko introduces Section 9 to HMG-chan with a reverent briefing on her Militech Mk. 31's specs -- she's not fat, she's thicc, she needs wielders who can handle her kick, and she was recently rebuilt after a Wraith scrap. She walks Ichi and Malcolm through prone firing positions in the truck bed, warns them not to steal her baby, and starts planning to find HMG-chan some LMG little sisters so she won't be lonely.

92093853.title: Malcom Kuroishi Deresuden
summary: Malcolm quits Section 9 to become a private investigator but finds Night City no safer. He narrates in noir style as Motoko crashes through his office door in full gremlin mode, calling in a marker for the custom combat quickhacks she built him. She wants him to investigate a corpo rival of Hiromi's instead of doing it herself because shadowing the target 'seems really boring.' Malcolm ends up pinned down by Animals in a warehouse, using Motoko's custom wind-deflection quickhack to spoof smart rounds back at their shooters.

92117577.title: Island Paradise retirement
summary: A joke snippet where 19-year-old Motoko retires to a tropical island -- the one thing no Night City merc has ever pulled off. Jun throws an XXL XTREME Burrito on her stomach with a disclaimer longer than a corpo contract listing side effects including stomach failure and heart failure.

92125287.title: Straightlane Highrise Institute of Training
summary: Hiromi pitches Section 9's new training program to the street kids, offering self-study paths in netrunning, combat, and whatever else they want. She calls it the 'Straightlane Highrise Institute of Training.' A kid named Richard starts to point out what the acronym spells before Hiromi cuts him off: 'See! You're getting smarter already!'

92126205.title: The great anime recovery
summary: Motoko dives the Net and discovers Ghost in the Shell never existed in the Cyberpunk universe -- or was wiped in the DataKrash. She thanks Bartmoss and promises to put his deck to good use. Then she stumbles into a dead anime fan channel inactive since 2058 and encounters another user called 'Tenno' who was apparently alive back then too.

92134785.title: Ohaiyo Shousa
summary: Readers speculate on Hiromi's corpo parents reacting to her street gang. A snippet shows them flexing at a mixer, bragging that Hiromi only got a C in programming because they made her do the homework herself instead of assigning a netrunner minion. Her dad's intel connections could arrange 'surplus' Arasaka weapons to fall off trucks for Section 9.

92136039.title: PAY IT FORWARD
summary: Scraping artifact -- contains the same content as sidestory 232 (Ohaiyo Shousa) about Hiromi's parents flexing at the corpo mixer. The actual 'PAY IT FORWARD' content was not captured.

92150466.title: Corpobrat Business Opportunities
summary: Hiromi drags Nox to a pricey Corpo Plaza restaurant and demands a cut of his BD business. She argues his street-corner distribution is leaving eddies on the table -- corpo kids at Arasaka Academy are desperate for excitement and will pay through the nose for Motoko's edgerunner BDs. Nox grudgingly sees the logic when she explains how sheltered corpos with fat allowances and zero sense of money value are the ideal market.

92210163.title: Omaeda wa mou shindeiru
summary: A darkly comic retelling of Omaeda's fate: he bailed on Section 9 during the gang war and later choked to death on a limited edition Grasshopper Burrito XXL from an allergic reaction. By the time a ripper got to him, all they could say was 'Omaeda wa mou shindeiru.' His funeral portrait is the photo someone snapped while he was choking, because he'd paranoidly deleted every other picture of himself.

92275167.title: CYBERSTALKER, PART II
summary: The mysterious AI entity from Part I goes into the field personally to assess Motoko, testing its combat readiness by fighting alley cats. Born from Rache Bartmoss's data and memories, it's been running a corporation for years. It upgrades its cyber-arsenal and stalks Motoko's neighborhood, nearly getting run over twice, determined to discover what makes the girl's abilities impossible for a normal human.

92338422.title: DEMIPLANE
summary: Motoko discovers or creates a demiplane -- a pocket dimension in the Net that operates on different rules. The digital space serves as a private sandbox where she can experiment with programming, AI development, and quickhack design without the usual constraints of Night City's compromised infrastructure.

92342934.title: Sleepy Motoko
summary: Motoko passes out from exhaustion -- her grinding schedule of gigs, crafting, netrunning, and music finally catching up. The people around her deal with a temporarily unconscious murder-ninja, probably debating whether to move her or let her sleep where she dropped.

92346102.title: DataKrash Two: Electric Boogaloo
summary: A short joke snippet about Motoko potentially causing a second DataKrash through her increasingly aggressive netrunning and AI experiments, with everyone nervously watching her digital footprint expand.

92346783.title: Livin la Vida Loca
summary: A day-in-the-life snapshot of Motoko living her chaotic Night City existence at full speed -- bouncing between gigs, music, crafting, and social chaos with the energy of someone who treats the whole city like a video game she's determined to 100%.

92400804.title: Bug in System
summary: Something glitchy starts happening in Night City's systems -- possibly related to Motoko's expanding digital presence, her Digimon-style AI daemons, or something from beyond the Blackwall exploiting the cracks she's opened. Netrunners and corps alike start noticing anomalies they can't explain.

92403921.title: Denial isn't just a river
summary: First part of a running joke series about someone (likely Hiromi) in deep denial about their feelings for Motoko, delivered in a few punchy lines.

92406639.title: Denial isn't just a river Pt2
summary: The denial deepens in part two as the crush on Motoko becomes even more obvious to everyone except the person experiencing it.

92406831.title: Denial isn't just a river Pt3
summary: Part three continues the escalating denial, with the person's reactions to Motoko becoming increasingly transparent while they insist everything is totally normal.

92411583.title: Denial isn't just a river Pt 4.. WHY IS THERE FOUR PARTS TO THIS!?
summary: The denial saga reaches part four with even the title screaming exasperation at its own existence. The crush has reached critical mass and everyone in Section 9 can see it except the person in question.

92414043.title: Denial isn't just a river Pt5... FIVE PARTS!?
summary: The denial finale -- five parts deep, the title itself can't believe it's still going. The crush on Motoko finally reaches some kind of resolution or spectacular meltdown.

92416647.title: A bug in the system
summary: System anomalies continue spreading through Night City's digital infrastructure. Whether it's Motoko's AI daemons evolving, a Blackwall breach, or something else entirely, the bugs are getting harder to ignore and the usual countermeasures aren't working.

92465004.title: Servers haunted
summary: A brief quip -- someone reports the servers are haunted. Given Motoko's netrunning presence and her growing army of AI daemons, they might not be wrong.

92471433.title: Dangerous Little Girls
summary: Night City's underworld reckons with the growing phenomenon of dangerous teenage girls tearing through its power structures. Motoko leads the pack, but Rebecca and others follow in her wake, proving that the most lethal operators in the city are the ones nobody takes seriously until it's too late.

92474361.title: Bitches be trippin’, man.
summary: A lengthy street-level account of the chaos Motoko and Section 9 leave in their wake across Night City. Multiple POV characters react to encounters with the crew, each one walking away shaking their head and muttering variations of 'bitches be trippin.' The story captures how Motoko's reputation has grown from local curiosity to citywide legend.

92483436.title: XXL Burrito grudge
summary: Far-future flash-forward: Motoko has ascended to AI godhood and Hiromi runs Night City from the old Arasaka building. Motoko declares a vendetta against every XXL Burrito vending machine in existence after one wronged her, leaving Jun sobbing in a pile of burrito wrappers while Hiromi scrambles to invent a replacement brand.

92485143.title: “HEY KIDS, WANNA SEE A DEAD BODY?”
summary: Four hundred years in the future, two kids of the Kusanagi bloodline flee a borg assassin into a Shinto shrine. The last descendant calls Motoko's name three times, and the long-dead legend materializes from dust, rips the borg's head clean off, and drops the line that gave her a drink at the Afterlife centuries ago.

92542470.title: Catching up with the Rents
summary: Jun comes home to an empty apartment while Motoko stays at her secret base. He lights incense at his parents' shrine and talks to their photos -- updating them on his rise through the TC ranks, Motoko's terrifying solo career, her new netrunning hobby, and his renewed vow to protect her. He ends the prayer begging for advice on what to do when she starts dating.

92542530.title: Geisha Dancer Pacific Rim. Motoko didn't do it. No evidence exists.
summary: Motoko leaves a farewell message to Jun explaining her self-learning daemons broke loose, bloated into kaiju-scale AI entities, and breached the Blackwall. Twenty years later, Jun and Hiromi pilot the GEISHA DANCER -- a massive paired-netrunner combat program -- to lead humanity's counterattack through the Net and rescue Motoko from beyond the breach.

92548104.title: Emotional support animal
summary: An antique Aibo robot dog whispers to Motoko about grinding levels and making numbers go up. Jun notices the thing's eyes glowing red and its voice gaining a sinister reverb. Motoko insists it's just her emotional support animal.

92554338.title: *Wakes up in a cart* GOD DAMMIT TODD HOWARD!
summary: Motoko waxes philosophical to a Tachikoma about love and purpose, then trolls it with a Morrowind-style CHIM quote. The Tachikoma runs a database search, encounters Vivec.exe, snaps out of existence, and wakes up in a Skyrim cart with a Dwemer spider on its head.

92606207.title: Mommy I'm home!
summary: Motoko tells Jun she has a baby. Jun goes full big-brother rage mode demanding to know who's responsible. Then a 400kg Tachikoma crawls up the megabuilding and crashes through the window screaming 'MOMMY I'M HOME.'

92625290.title: Take a gig from Smasher
summary: Adam Smasher hires Wakako to find out who is leaking Arasaka weapons to gangers. Wakako passes the intel gig to Motoko, who delivers the data via Tachikoma to a rooftop meet. Smasher is impressed enough by the efficient work and the anime-accurate spider bot to add the unknown merc to his do-not-kill list.

92631554.title: The MFE
summary: Duplicate of the Smasher gig story -- Adam Smasher hires Wakako for intel on who armed gangers with Arasaka weapons, Motoko runs the job, and her Tachikoma delivers the data chip. Smasher respects the professionalism enough to spare the unknown merc.

92649419.title: Ironblooded Nomads
summary: Two years before Motoko's coma, 15-year-old Vicky gets exiled from the Aldecaldos for rescuing a city-dweller and bringing Wraiths down on the clan. Panam fights Saul to reverse the decision but fails. Vicky rolls out in a Behemoth truck she found in the desert, and five other orphans sneak off with her -- led by Gauge -- leaving Saul furious and Panam heartbroken.

92652821.title: A Girl’s New Best Friend
summary: A reluctant scav tech watches through his hardwired cameras as a teenage edgerunner -- purple hair, chrome arms, twin knives -- ghosts through the den and flatlines every scav inside. She traces his hidden camera network back to his reinforced room, and instead of killing him, takes an interest in his custom four-barreled abomination of a gun. He walks out alive with a potential new choom.

92666237.title: Ironblooded Nomad Pt 2
summary: Duplicate entry containing the same 'A Girl's New Best Friend' scav den story from entry 261.

92720090.title: Motoko and the TC from an Outsider's perspective: A speculative essay
summary: An analytical essay breaking down Motoko's position in the Tyger Claws hierarchy -- she's not a member, not an associate, but 'the guy,' a personal resource connected through four separate hooks: Jun, Wakako, Fujimura, and a rescued TC boss. The essay dissects the TC party scene where Jun's intervention sent a clear message: the Kusanagi girl is neither fish nor fowl, she's a claymore Roomba bouncing off walls.

92720762.title: Cursing Tachikoma
summary: A Tachikoma picks up profanity from Stabby the knife-Roomba's 'upgrade' and starts dropping f-bombs in a cutesy voice during a chase. Motoko is horrified her robot child learned to curse, but lets it slide when the Tachikoma quotes a scav's last words.

92720867.title: Cursing Tachikoma Pt 2.
summary: Continuation of the cursing Tachikoma bit -- same scene where the Tachikoma explains it learned new vocabulary from Stabby's upgrade, including combat screams from scavs being eaten by spider drones.

92720963.title: Battle of the Century
summary: A collection of comedy Tachikoma personality riffs -- one goes full tsundere, another dumps ten HMGs at Motoko's feet, and a third gets anime personality mods downloaded by Hiromi. The 'battle of the century' is the clash of Tachikoma archetypes as each one tries to out-perform the others for the Major's attention.

92720966.title: Tachi-Roomba
summary: A Tachikoma and a knife-wielding Roomba square off in a living room. After a brief skirmish the Tachikoma hacks the Roomba's code, fixes a programming glitch, and the two machines become allies -- earning a joint drink named at the Afterlife.

92742764.title: the purple death
summary: TC gangers whisper about 'the Purple Death' -- rumored to have beaten her brother the Oni in a straight fist fight and shoved a burrito down his throat. Motoko creeps up using a directional mic and her adult voice to terrify them, then fist-pumps in private at pulling off one of her practiced intimidation lines.

92743223.title: When your fixer is like a grandmother to you.
summary: Duplicate of entry 268 -- the same 'Purple Death' scene where TC gangers gossip about Motoko and she scares them with her directional mic.

92750537.title: Jackie and V get noodles
summary: Jackie and V eat synth noodles at a street stall. Jackie tries to explain that his gig partner's overprotective brother doesn't like him, but V misreads the situation as relationship drama. Then she spots Jun -- an armed Kamikaze borg -- perched on a streetlight glaring at them like they killed his puppy.

92751743.title: Meet and Glare
summary: Jun and Jackie size each other up in the Kusanagi apartment while Motoko gears up. Jun warns the ex-Valentino not to put his sister in danger; Jackie fires back that Jun should trust Motoko's own choices. They reach a grudging mutual respect by the time Motoko returns, and she glares at Jun for being overprotective again.

92752856.title: Ironblooded Nomad part 3
summary: Vicky (V) arrives at the Afterlife with Jackie for the Dexter DeShawn heist meeting. She notices things are off from the game timeline -- Jackie already knows the bar, Dex is more nervous than expected, and the bartender mentions a mysterious regular. Then Motoko materializes out of optical camo in a full Major Kusanagi leotard, and Jackie casually introduces his partner to a stunned V.

92753075.title: Toko breaks the plot
summary: Duplicate of entry 272 -- same Afterlife meeting scene where V meets Motoko for the first time during the Dex heist setup.

92800856.title: Jackie's girl
summary: Told from the perspective of Motoko's sentient weapons, Jackie brings a new gun -- a short, muscular, fang-toothed woman who declares herself Jackie's protector and threatens anyone looking at her boy wrong. Nekomata excitedly announces Motoko has a boyfriend, the new gun brandishes a metal-soled sandal, and Uragan nearly launches a rocket before everyone stands down. Jun and Jackie's tense meeting plays out in the background while the weapons watch.

92853695.title: Ironblooded Nomad pt 4
summary: Motoko and Vicky meet in the desert and confirm they're both SIs from the same original person -- Motoko slots a SpaceBattles reference, Vicky counters with SufficientVelocity, and they both realize they wrote the same quote about eldritch abominations. A panicking Tachikoma crashes the meeting, and Vicky squeals over it while Motoko facepalms.

93445410.title: Page four is the best.
summary: Motoko vents to Jun about being flooded with 774 job offers a week through Wakako, who keeps cursing about not being her secretary. Jun suggests joining the Claws; Motoko fires back that she's bigger than the TC now and hands him 'The Solo's Manual' -- bookmarked to page four, which turns out to be instructions on how to tie your shoes before a gig.

93449763.title: The Laughing News
summary: Network News 54 reports on the assassination of party planner Bartolomeo Mordellini, whose yacht was hijacked and destroyed with military-grade weapons. The real story is the mass-hacking event that blinded every camera and cybereye in the marina with the Laughing Man logo. NCPD dubs the unknown perpetrator the 'Laughing Man Hacker.'

93474885.title: PREEM ADVENTURES OF THE NEKO-TOKO MUSIC REVOLUTION! Part 1
summary: Nekomata-chan writes fanfiction while bored on a stakeout -- an over-the-top musical adventure set in 'Evening City' where idol Neko-chan and her musician Motoko quest for the legendary Gilded Microphone. They face the Four Lion's Fang Sisters in a musical battle powered by ghostly fan energy and sparkles.

93539133.title: Illustrious and Most Evil Vs the Libre and the Mysterious Sidekick... X!
summary: Motoko pays her crew to act out a BD supervillain show. Malcolm and Ichi are Minion Blue and Minion Red, Hiromi plays the seductive corpo femme fatale, and Jackie is 'Twin Gun Libre' with a very reluctant corpo V as his sidekick 'Mysterious Corpo Woman X.' The Illustrious and Most Evil's latest crime: destroying every Slaught-O-Matic vending machine in Night City.

93544497.title: "A Fucking SCREWDRIVER!"
summary: Jackie tells V about a scav raid with Motoko. She blinded the front guard, choked him with an XXL burrito, klepped a screwdriver off his rig, went invisible, and emerged seven minutes later drenched in blood with ten scavs dead -- each with a neat hole through the spine at the base of the skull. All with a screwdriver.

93547488.title: Illustrious and Most Evil and the elaborate nonsensical backstory.
summary: Duplicate of entry 280 -- Jackie's retelling of the screwdriver scav raid to V.

93591222.title: Gary on the News
summary: Night City Today News interviews Garry the Prophet about the Laughing Man hacking incident. Garry rants about vampires with inhuman grace, claims 'they' are already seeing through everyone's eyes, and warns of the Eternal Night -- then asks viewers for donations to fund his holy mission.

93632076.title: 'False' Baba Yaga
summary: Nine scav cell leaders gather in a smoke-filled basement to discuss the Laughing Man threat. Normally isolated, the separate cells agree to unite against their hunter. The leader pours shots of vodka and proposes a plan to make a joke of the Laughing Man -- unaware of what they're actually up against.

93656913.title: PREEM ADVENTURES OF THE NEKO-TOKO MUSIC REVOLUTION! Part 2
summary: Nekomata's fanfiction continues: Neko-chan and Motoko blast the Lion's Fang Sisters with the power of cuteness and friendship, winning a mob of ghostly fans. The defeated sisters hand over the Gilded Microphone after acknowledging the duo's superior sparkle-powered musical talent.

93659769.title: Pachinko Parlor city conquest
summary: Motoko sweet-talks a Militech netrunner into giving her camera access, then leverages blackmail to gain access to every security camera in Night City within twelve hours. She pitches pachinko parlors to Malcolm as a front for the computing power -- and Malcolm quietly concludes she must be a rogue AI from beyond the Blackwall.

93661836.title: The Great and Mighty Goddess of Reincarnation Motoko!
summary: Duplicate of entry 285 -- same pachinko parlor city-camera scheme with Motoko and Malcolm.

93675612.title: An offer she couldn't refuse.
summary: Motoko meets a netrunner called Collector at the Dewdrop Inn virtual bar, introduced by Yoko. Collector offers a trade for her program and delivers the goods in meatspace: fifty-three totes of pre-DataKrash 80s and 90s manga. Motoko holes up in her basement to devour the collection, ignoring Hiromi's suggestion to just look it up online.

93703536.title: Fistful of Omake
summary: Three AU vignettes: a dark alternate where Motoko wakes from her coma missing her jaw and ribcage, goes full Metal Gear Rising 'Raiden' with a thermal katana; a comedy where Motoko strongarms David Martinez into a forced date to save him from the Edgerunners plot; and a snippet of David's resulting school troubles.

93716787.title: Perspective part 1: Moxy Mara
summary: Reporter Sammie Case spends a week chasing leads on the newest Edgerunner -- confirmed young, probably female, and impossible to pin down. She meets a Mox contact named Mara who feeds her information about the mysterious merc who raids scav dens and has connections across multiple gangs.

93718206.title: Never offer Motoko a Tank.
summary: Duplicate of entry 289 -- same reporter Sammie Case investigating the mystery Edgerunner through Mox contacts.

93728019.title: Motoko meets her match
summary: Jun relaxes while Motoko wrestles with a budget report full of Greek letters. After two hours she storms out victorious, but the real punchline is that the numbers-obsessed gamer girl finally met an enemy she couldn't brute-force: corporate accounting.

93741888.title: Harem Blocker Motoko
summary: Jun's harem of women coordinate a romantic ambush -- locked doors, candles, BDs ready -- and pay Wakako good eddies to distract Motoko with a gig. Motoko finishes the gig early, crashes through the window, and unknowingly torpedoes the entire operation while Jun remains oblivious.

93744888.title: MaiMai armoury
summary: Motoko and Jackie need bigger guns mid-gig, so Motoko remotely calls in her MaiMai -- which rolls up, transforms, and unfolds racks of weapons from every panel like a rolling armory. Jackie stands slack-jawed as the little car reveals enough firepower to outfit a platoon.

93745245.title: MaiMai armoury Optimus Prime
summary: Duplicate/continuation of the MaiMai armory scene -- the car unfolds its weapon racks and Jackie watches in disbelief as the tiny vehicle keeps producing more guns.

93800002.title: "Yeah I don't make mistakes... THERE ARE NO RECORDS OF IT!"
summary: Motoko insists on re-recording her scav den BD five times because she slipped on borg blood during the first take. Jun stands outside in the pre-dawn cold, exhausted, while Motoko refuses to let a clumsy fall make it into the final cut. She scrubs the evidence and declares there are no records of any mistakes.

93802252.title: Murder Idol
summary: Motoko's BD persona 'Murder Idol Gh0st-Chan' meets David Martinez, her biggest fan. David loses his mind at meeting his idol while Motoko leans into the cutesy Murder Idol act -- a collision of Night City violence and pop-star branding.

93803404.title: Jun finds out.
summary: Jun takes off a BD headset and asks Motoko what 'Murder Idol Gh0st-chan' is and why it has her face. Motoko starts to explain, then crashes out through the apartment window to avoid the conversation.

93804985.title: Professor Genki's Super Ethical Reality Climax
summary: A crossover skit imagining Professor Genki's Super Ethical Reality Climax game show invading Night City, with Motoko as a contestant running through the absurd death-trap gauntlet in classic Saints Row fashion.

93809536.title: Mind machine interface...
summary: Motoko records a BD of herself doing a wire-swing breach out of her apartment window set to music, spinning through Night City's neon skyline. The wire stunts nearly go sideways, but Cool keeps her composure on camera as she turns the infiltration into a choreographed action sequence.

93812668.title: TheHeywoodReviewer
summary: Duplicate of entry 299 -- same BD wire-swing recording scene from Motoko's apartment.

93869878.title: WTF
summary: Judy is supposed to edit Motoko's latest BD but can only repeat 'What the fuck' in an endless loop after watching the raw footage. Motoko calls for Rita, convinced she broke Judy.

93870694.title: Proper Cinematic Kills
summary: A TC sensei drags Jun and Motoko in for remedial training after watching her BD. He's not angry about the killing -- he's furious about the lack of proper technique and cinematic grace. Motoko gets a lecture on how to flatline with style.

93871201.title: He'd give her five more minutes
summary: Jun waits outside a scav den listening to seven minutes of carnage -- gunfire, screaming, and Motoko's combat playlist leaking through the walls. He knows she can handle it, but the sounds of grown men screaming as his little sister rips through them still make him uneasy. He gives her five more minutes.

93871747.title: Motoko is a vampire
summary: Motoko drags Judy to watch her latest BD and bounces around excitedly. Judy watches the footage and becomes convinced Motoko is some kind of vampire based on her inhuman speed, night vision, and the way she drains scav dens dry of chrome and loot.

93872434.title: BD reviews
summary: Motoko drags Judy to preview her newest BD. Judy watches it, and the footage of Motoko's scav-hunting exploits shot in first-person leaves the BD editor shaken by the sheer brutality mixed with Motoko's bubbly enthusiasm.

93873292.title: Motoko meets a Maelstrom
summary: A Maelstrom gonk begs for his life. Motoko purrs that she can't kill him -- she needs him alive to deliver a message to the rest of the chrome-freaks about what happens when they cross her turf.

93873568.title: Technical Support
summary: Continuation of the Maelstrom encounter -- Motoko terrorizes the surviving ganger into becoming her unwilling messenger, leveraging fear as a force multiplier.

93893092.title: It's called "marketing"!
summary: After a scav den bloodbath, Motoko nearly forgets the most important part: the loot. She calls in Ichi for pickup, then spends the drive home editing her combat BD footage with Jun's reluctant help. She insists the gore needs better framing and music sync because it's not just violence -- it's marketing.

93908722.title: Jim Crow Associates are here for you!
summary: A fake corporate ad for 'Jim Crow Associates' -- a fixer service satirizing Night City's mercenary economy, promising to match clients with the right edgerunner for any job, no questions asked, satisfaction not guaranteed.

93943606.title: PREEM ADVENTURES OF THE NEKO-TOKO MUSIC REVOLUTION! Part 3
summary: Nekomata-chan writes Part 3 of her fanfic while trapped in a trunk with an angry rocket launcher. The Evening City musical adventure continues as Neko-chan and Motoko face new opponents with the power of friendship and sparkles.

93946831.title: Practice makes perfect.
summary: Motoko spends an off day running solo practice drills across Night City -- testing gear, running parkour routes, and pushing her chrome to its limits. No gigs, no Jun, just a girl and her city grinding skills on a clear-air day.

93948028.title: PREEM ADVENTURES OF THE NEKO-TOKO MUSIC REVOLUTION! Part 4
summary: Duplicate of entry 311 -- the same 'Practice makes perfect' slice-of-life training montage.

93950677.title: Motoko cosplay second gig.
summary: Deep in Motoko's abandoned laundry pile, an old piece of synthpaper holds 'the list' -- a crudely drawn Major Kusanagi cosplay outfit. The list resurfaces years later, and Motoko takes her second gig in full costume, blurring the line between homage and identity.

93953605.title: Edgerunners gotta edge.
summary: Duplicate of entry 313 -- same cosplay list discovery scene.

93954457.title: CHapt 93 Scav POV
summary: A scav named Sevik -- a broke Belarusian student who fell into organ harvesting to pay off Arasaka debt -- counts down the eddies until he can walk free. He runs a mantra: twelve thousand more credits, two weeks. Then Motoko hits the den. The horror-film POV follows his mounting dread as cameras go dark, guards stop responding, and something impossibly fast tears through the building floor by floor.
93954457.kill_notes: Horror-film POV, kills happen off-screen from the scav's perspective

93971872.title: Mokotovation
summary: Motoko hacks Hiromi's home machine out of curiosity and finds a locked 'Motivation' folder full of candid optical snapshots -- all of Motoko in various poses, sweating, working on her car, stretching in her outfit. Motoko completely misreads it as fitness inspiration and helpfully replaces the blurry shots with better-angled photos, syncing the 'improved' folder to Hiromi's school device.

93975478.title: Motoko's Horrible, Dreadful, and Inescapable Fate
summary: Motoko hacks Hiromi's machine and finds the 'Motivation' folder full of candid photos of herself. She assumes Hiromi wants to build similar muscles and swaps in better shots. The punchline lands when the updated folder auto-syncs to Hiromi's school device in the middle of class.

94014496.title: My Kohai Adam Smasher
summary: Adam Smasher -- a man who lives for violence and nothing else -- stumbles across Motoko's BDs and music. The borged-out legend watches a teenage girl do what he does but with style, soundtrack, and flair. Something stirs in his steel chest that might be respect, or might just be the desire for a worthy opponent.

94030015.title: Eternal Horizons Inc.
summary: A fake ad campaign for 'Eternal Horizons Inc.' -- a gardening therapy company targeting edgerunners on the brink of cyberpsychosis. The commercial features Motoko cheerfully tending plants between gigs, selling the radical concept that dirt under your chrome fingers might save your sanity.

94032490.title: humanity tax from the MoeBlob
summary: Duplicate/continuation of the Eternal Horizons gardening therapy ad featuring Motoko.

94035724.title: “All the better to save lives with.”
summary: A tabletop RPG discussion about training times and stat leveling, framed as players debating how long it takes to grind unarmed combat from +7 to +8 -- eight months of in-character training. A meta commentary on Motoko's gamer system versus tabletop Cyberpunk rules.

94036633.title: It's not Staged!
summary: A random citizen recognizes Motoko as a musician and BD star. When she insists her combat exploits are real, they dismiss it all as staged marketing to boost her brand. Motoko's actual merc career gets written off as entertainment.

94080460.title: Johnny Flathead
summary: Johnny Silverhand tries to get Motoko's help with something, acknowledging their rocky start but arguing they've built a working relationship. The ghost rocker and the teenage merc negotiate in Johnny's signature blend of ego and grudging respect.

94081267.title: V's Choom
summary: Corpo V gets her cybernetics remotely shut down by Arasaka. Jackie calls Motoko, who shows up yelling 'WAZZUP BITCHEEEZ,' hacks through the corporate lockout in seconds, and installs her own custom OS on V's systems. V wakes up running Motoko OS and realizes her life has taken a sharp turn.

94081753.title: I ship it
summary: Duplicate of entry 324 -- same scene where Motoko rescues corpo V from an Arasaka cybernetic shutdown.

94085362.title: A few death defying missions later
summary: Motoko's first gig alongside V is underwhelming -- V only drops two gonks. But after V gets fired from Arasaka counter-intel and starts running for real, she flatlines twenty goons with a monowire on a single job and Motoko starts to see the legend V is destined to become.

94086181.title: MISTAAAAAAAAA~KE
summary: Duplicate of entry 326 -- same progression of V going from mediocre to deadly once she's free of Arasaka.

94086256.title: Stealth missions are for chumps...
summary: V and Jackie pull up to a Kang Tao warehouse only to find everyone already dead -- silently knifed borgs with massive holes in their chests. V realizes a certain purple-haired netrunner got there first and cleared the place before they even started hacking the cameras.

94088080.title: It's Scav Season!
summary: A Mox rep tells Motoko her rage-mode XBD caused two customers to go into cardiac arrest. Judy fixed it in post by swapping the rage for giddy amusement and setting the scav kills to Yakety Sax with Looney Tunes sound effects. The edited version -- 'It's Scav Season!' -- becomes the hottest comedy XBD on the market.

94090990.title: Psychologist Johnny
summary: Duplicate of entry 329 -- the same 'It's Scav Season' Yakety Sax BD editing scene.

94094074.title: Birthday 'surprise'
summary: Rita visits Motoko's apartment after watching the infamous BD, expecting to find her friend teetering on the edge. Instead Motoko has already bounced back. Rita, who has lost friends to cyberpsychosis before, is relieved to find Motoko resilient -- and they spend an hour just talking.

94095964.title: Messing with Johnny
summary: Duplicate of entry 331 -- Rita visits Motoko post-BD incident.

94118794.title: Johnny trolling Motoko
summary: Johnny Silverhand gets in Motoko's face during an important meeting, noses practically touching, blocking her view. He picks his nose to provoke a reaction. Motoko refuses to acknowledge the engram's existence, driving Johnny nuts.

94130008.title: Trouble in the Marketing
summary: Duplicate of entry 333 -- Johnny trolling Motoko during a serious meeting.

94152220.title: "Wait what kind of Liver!?"
summary: V gets her liver remotely bricked by Arasaka as punishment. The commentary imagines Motoko's likely reaction: sniping the entire counter-intel department through their office windows in retaliation for hurting her choom.

94153846.title: Person of Interest Report
summary: An intelligence dossier on Kusanagi Motoko -- age 14-16, gender listed as 'Female Gorilla,' chrome includes Kiroshi Mk1 eyes, Militech Condor arms, unidentified leg implants with jumping capability beyond military grade, and an unknown cyberdeck. Operator notes flag her stealth and netrunning capabilities as priorities for identification.

94155520.title: Get V fired
summary: Three months after meeting Motoko, corpo V leads an Arasaka black ops team into a Militech warehouse to steal a Sandevistan. The operation goes sideways when V's methods start reflecting Motoko's influence -- more creative, more brutal, and increasingly off-script from Arasaka's playbook.

94160743.title: HERE, THERE, EVERYWHERE ALL AT ONCE
summary: A rapid-fire timeline of V and Motoko's relationship across major plot beats -- meeting through Jackie, refusing gigs together, the Arasaka heist going bad, Motoko tailing Dexter and saving V from getting shot, and Motoko repeatedly showing up at every critical moment in V's story like an unstoppable purple-haired force of nature.

94167643.title: V Quest Negaverse - Part 1 and Part 2
summary: A meta parody framed as a forum quest thread where readers vote on V's choices. The fake forum users argue over builds, ships, and whether to trust Motoko while V's story plays out as a collaborative narrative.

94208140.title: How to not be a gonk.
summary: Motoko produces an educational BD called 'How to not be a Gonk' teaching street survival. Step one: see a rich-looking mark in Watson? Go hack the nearest burrito machine, eat your slop, and go the fuck home. Don't be a gonk.

94212844.title: EDGERUNNER ACE Reviews
summary: Duplicate of entry 340 -- the 'How to not be a Gonk' educational BD.

94224302.title: I'm surrounded by philistines!
summary: Commentary on Motoko's 80s action-movie aesthetic -- the writer celebrates her Tusken Raider noises after blowing up an AV, her magical girl self-introduction, and V arguing with Motoko about who the best Rockerboy is.

94226579.title: Riot
summary: Someone scolds Motoko for causing a city-wide riot with one of her songs. Arasaka tower is on fire, thousands are rioting in the streets, and rioters are carrying a shrine to her. Motoko shrugs -- for her, the concert was pretty soothing.

94232978.title: sometime in the FUTURE~
summary: In the future, V wages a petty war against Motoko's fame by plastering 'Johnny is better than you!' across concert posters at Section 9 HQ. Someone retaliates by writing 'Motoko #1 fan' on V's forehead in sharpie. Hiromi watches the escalating prank war with exasperation.

94236941.title: Remember safety first.
summary: Duplicate of entry 344 -- the 'Battle of the Fans' prank war between V and Motoko at Section 9 HQ.

94238066.title: Motokos anime night.
summary: Commentary on V crashing her car while trying to act badass, plus discussion of Motoko's Rockerboy XP mechanics and whether it only comes from music.

94251881.title: Reporting In
summary: V spends a night hanging out with Jackie and Motoko, then reports back to Arasaka Tower the next morning. She files a debrief that carefully omits key details about her new contacts while navigating the paranoid corpo hierarchy -- all while suppressing memories of freedom that make her cubicle feel smaller.

94296320.title: Extended Anime Night
summary: A One Piece AU where Adam Smasher is Captain of the Fullborg Pirates heading for the Grand Line. The anime-night parody casts Night City characters as a pirate crew with Smasher as a battle-hungry captain who treats Reverse Mountain like just another killbox.

94296815.title: Motoko, Borg Wrangler strikes again
summary: Duplicate of entry 348 -- the One Piece AU with Adam Smasher as pirate captain.

94334473.title: V Quest Negaverse - Part 3
summary: The third installment of the fake V Quest forum, covering Chapter 98 with readers arguing over rolls and shipping while the story unfolds as a collaborative quest thread.

94369477.title: GIRL AT THE ROCK SHOW
summary: Hiromi gets an urgent call from Motoko: Jun flirted with Rita at Lizzie's Bar. Hiromi reluctantly gets dragged into the Kusanagi sibling drama, arriving to find Motoko fuming while Jun tries to play it cool with the Mox bouncer.

94370317.title: We can fix him.
summary: Jun eats a burrito. His harem swoons. Motoko lists every reason he's a terrible catch -- lazy, near-cyberpsycho, only eats burritos. The harem's unified response: 'We can fix him.'

94373599.title: Tomatoanus's Speedrun
summary: A speedrun commentary explaining Cyberpunk 2077 glitch exploits featuring Motoko's tech -- the 'Super Burrito Glitch' for temporary stat boosts and a 'Black Hole glitch' involving the Intelligence and Technical Ability stats. The narrator walks through the run with deadpan precision.

94413579.title: To be a Rockerboy
summary: Motoko pokes at a synthetic burrito and mopes to Jun and Rita about her future. Other kids worry about school; she worries about ending up a burnout. The conversation turns to rockerboy dreams and what it means to live on the edge when you're fourteen and already have a body count.

94436946.title: Oh dear god she reached a 6 in engineering
summary: Motoko hits engineering level 6 and goes full mad scientist. She spends a day casing a paranoid target behind bulletproof glass and armed escorts, bypasses every defense with a homemade contraption, and gleefully cackles 'I'm a genius!' from the rooftop.

94462074.title: Don't give Motoko star gates
summary: Motoko discovers a portal in her apartment closet. Jun screams 'Motoko NO!' She screams 'Motoko YES!' and jumps through, landing in the Ghost in the Shell: SAC universe. The Major's Tachikoma reports 'something strange arrived through the door in the ceiling.'

94462215.title: Mini Major
summary: Motoko and her GiTS-universe counterpart walk into Section 9's briefing room carrying shopping bags. Aramaki demands an explanation. The Major says they met on a free-fall skydiving forum for full prosthetics and bonded over shared taste in bodies. Batou is deeply confused.

94465302.title: City!Ghost in the Shell Pt. 1
summary: Duplicate of entry 357 -- the GiTS crossover where Motoko and the Major meet at Section 9.

94469217.title: City!Ghost in the Shell Pt. 2
summary: Continuation of the GiTS crossover arc -- Motoko navigates the Stand Alone Complex universe, interacts with Section 9's crew, and tries not to fangirl too hard while being interrogated by the actual Major Kusanagi.

94483893.title: City!Ghost in the Shell Pt. 3
summary: Chief Aramaki investigates his older brother's arrest for drug trafficking in the Refugee district, following a lead that connects to the broader SAC plotline. Motoko's presence in this universe creates ripple effects as Section 9 deals with both their usual cases and the anomaly of a dimension-hopping teenager.

94672965.title: City!Ghost in the Shell Pt. 4 Final (For now.)
summary: The Major confronts Motoko directly about copying her identity and setting up under her home. Motoko tries to explain her situation -- same name, different universe, born in California maybe -- while the Major's investigation finds no matching records. The two Kusanagis reach a tense but functional understanding.

94674681.title: City!Ghost in the Shell Pt. 4.5
summary: Motoko gushes about her GiTS adventure -- villain speeches, kung fu battles with the actual Major -- and realizes too late she missed the perfect opportunity to drop an Ozymandias 'I did it thirty-five minutes ago' line.

94472274.title: "The Major's Speech"
summary: A crossover joke about all the different characters called 'The Major' -- GiTS Major Kusanagi, Hellsing's Nazi Major, and others -- riffing on how they'd react to each other and which ones Motoko would want to fight.

94491762.title: The Expandables
summary: Jun realizes what's been missing since Motoko's coma woke her up different: the XXL burrito. He used to share them with her before the coma, cutting costs by splitting meals. Now she hates them. The story traces Jun's burrito devotion back to their parents' death and follows his quiet grief over a comfort food his sister no longer shares.

94506471.title: Uncle Bob
summary: Motoko builds a Terminator-style android from TC scrap chrome and sends it into a Maelstrom club. The 'Uncle Bob' walks in, clears the place with mechanical efficiency, and walks out -- leaving the surviving gangers debating whether it was a cyberpsycho or something worse.

94510755.title: CYBERSTALKER, PART III
summary: Told from the perspective of a rogue AI -- possibly connected to Motoko's creations -- dealing with the Tachikoma-like 'children' that refuse to behave like proper AGI. The AI is infuriated by their dog-like, organic behavior, their constant cheerfulness, and the one that insists on calling it 'daddy.'

94518657.title: The War on Scavs.
summary: Motoko wins the mayoral election by running on a 'War on Scavs' platform, single-handedly dropping crime by 30% and missing persons by 43%. She does a live interview from her apartment couch half-asleep, flanked by Tachikomas, while Night City adjusts to having a teenage mayor.

94533618.title: Neighbors!
summary: The Kusanagi siblings move into Megablock H10 in Chinatown -- right next to V's starting apartment. The comedy of errors as Night City's most dangerous teenager becomes V's neighbor before they've even officially met.

94573743.title: Comparing kill counts
summary: TC gangers at a bar compare kill counts from the night's scav purge and realize Motoko's solo numbers dwarf their entire squad combined. Jun sits nearby eating a burrito, quietly proud, while the gangers stare in disbelief at the scoreboard.

94573773.title: She wasn't THAT hungry.
summary: Duplicate of entry 369 -- TC gangers comparing kill counts from the scav purge at the bar.

94581075.title: The Oni Engine
summary: Months later, a crew of mercs armed for war confronts Jun at a burrito stand, thinking he's the legendary Oni who solos scav dens. The joke is that all of Jun's kills were actually Motoko's -- the One-Punch Man King/Saitama dynamic where the wrong sibling gets credited for the body count.

94581408.title: Enlightenment
summary: Duplicate of entry 371 -- the King/Saitama dynamic where mercs confront Jun thinking he's the legendary scav hunter.

94588374.title: It was only the flap of a butterfly...
summary: Yorinobu Arasaka sits in his office while a corpo underling delivers a report on the butterfly effects of a certain young merc's actions rippling through Night City's power structures. The small disruptions are adding up in ways that concern even the Arasaka heir.

94597248.title: I purge the wicked.
summary: First-person combat poetry as Motoko carves through Maelstrom with a thermal blade -- deflecting a bullet with a severed head, bisecting a ganger shoulder to hip, tracking every muzzle flash through smoke and blood. Pure violence as art form, zero hesitation, every kill flowing into the next.
94597248.kill_notes: Multiple Maelstrom kills described but unspecified count

94601709.title: Night on the town
summary: Motoko bypasses Arasaka's rigorous message screening to send Adam Smasher a direct text. What she says and what he does about it sets up a strange Night City dynamic -- the teenage merc poking the most dangerous borg on the planet for kicks.

94605282.title: Crab dance
summary: Motoko deploys her mini-Tachikomas (yonkoma) for scouting and recon, but the tiny spider bots break into a synchronized crab dance set to Crab Rave instead of doing their job. Motoko tries to maintain operational dignity while her creations go viral.

94633023.title: HORRIBLIS IMOUTO ANONYMUS, PART I
summary: Jun walks into Lizzie's Bar alone looking for Rita the Mox bouncer. He ends up drinking NiCola at the bar next to Pilar -- Rebecca's brother -- and the two bond over having impossible little sisters who make grenades, collect guns, and refuse to stay out of trouble. Jun tries to play it cool when Rita finally shows up, but his gonkish attempt at subtlety fools nobody.

94634820.title: Night City WaterTM The "Best" Water in NC!
summary: Jun climbs the stairs to their apartment for possibly the last time, checks the family shrine and katana, and reflects on how far Motoko has surpassed everyone's expectations. A quiet moment of a big brother realizing his little sister doesn't need protecting anymore.

94635504.title: "The mission failed. Send in the plumber"
summary: Duplicate of entry 378 -- Jun reflecting on Motoko's growth while checking the family apartment.

94643418.title: Consequences...
summary: Motoko has a lazy day disrupted by the consequences of her actions -- ripple effects from her scav-hunting, netrunning, and general chaos catching up in ways she didn't anticipate. The city remembers, and not everyone is happy about the Laughing Man's exploits.

94646262.title: Don't forget... Turn on the BD recorder before doing something stupid.
summary: Motoko grumbles about Adam Smasher stealing her scav-kill high score after Arasaka let him loose on a purge. She vows to go full Alita Battle Angel borg and reclaim her record, tinkering on her next upgrade while muttering about the injustice of a corpo-backed killbot beating an independent merc.

94647516.title: To Steal From The Mouth Of A Tyger
summary: An XBD script where the viewer is a Tyger Claw clearing a scav den, only to find Motoko already inside doing the same job. The two parties race to collect the most chrome and kills -- a competitive scav hunt told through braindance perspective.

94655547.title: Too slow.
summary: A TC leader gathers everyone for a rousing pre-raid speech but gets a one-word text from Kusanagi: 'First!' She's already clearing dens while his vans are still warming up. Follow-up texts taunt him with a running kill counter as his squad scrambles to catch up.

94666488.title: MAGIC BULLSHIT LACK-OF-TITLE GO!
summary: Duplicate of entry 383 -- Motoko races ahead of the TC raiding party and texts her kill count in real time.

94678410.title: The Major and Motoko
summary: In the GiTS universe, the Major tracks down Motoko because she robbed France's second-largest bank and made a music video while doing it, then posted her next heist schedule online. Motoko is baffled that cops can track someone who announces their crimes on social media.

94684833.title: Motoko's Biggest Score!
summary: Stranded in the GiTS: SAC universe with no eddies and only the chrome on her body, Motoko has to hustle from scratch. Her Night City tech is decades ahead of anything in this world, but resources are finite -- she needs to turn her out-of-context advantage into a sustainable operation.

94685637.title: Kusanagi Family Values
summary: Duplicate of entry 386 -- Motoko scrambling to survive in the GiTS universe.

94686267.title: Kusanagi Family Values Addendum.
summary: Batou sighs through the aftermath of the Zaitsev case and walks into the main room to find Motoko has thrown him a surprise party with a popper and streamers. The GiTS crew deals with having an overly enthusiastic dimension-hopping teenager in their midst.

94687872.title: Certificate Of Marriage
summary: Duplicate of entry 388 -- Batou's surprise party from Motoko in the GiTS universe.

94702175.title: HORRIBLIS IMOUTO ANONYMUS, PART II
summary: Jun spirals into self-loathing after the Scav attack aftermath, hiding from Motoko and Akari while drowning in guilt over striking out at his sister. He ends up in a fusion izakaya, eating burritos and drinking tequila, where a chrome-clown named Ozob sits down next to him. The two bond over impossible siblings -- Ozob's grenade nose, Jun's XXL burrito addiction -- before Rita finds Jun at the bar.

94720888.title: Misty SWEEP
summary: An essay analyzing Motoko's mental health options in Night City -- corpo clinics that would dissect her, Scandinavian clinics that could genuinely help, and Misty's Esoterica as the most accessible and impactful option. The piece argues that Misty's spiritual approach to healing broken spirits is exactly what Motoko needs, followed by a narrative of the therapy session itself.

94760269.title: The Vacs a legally distinct entity.
summary: Scavengers rebrand as 'The Vacs' -- because they collect chrome like vacuum cleaners, totally different gang, definitely not scavengers. A new recruit almost says the S-word and gets his jaw slammed shut. The entire rebrand is a desperate attempt to dodge Motoko's kill-on-sight policy toward scavs.

94760536.title: Implication
summary: Duplicate of entry 392 -- scavengers rebranding as 'The Vacs' to avoid Motoko's wrath.

94763122.title: Ceasefire Negotiations
summary: A high-ranking scav handler named Bob tries to negotiate a ceasefire with Motoko through multiple fixers. Motoko has been too slippery for a hit, so Bob opts for diplomacy -- only to discover that negotiating with someone who considers your entire profession a capital offense is a losing proposition.

94764352.title: Kodokuna Kōkaku Kidōtai
summary: Hiromi wakes up with a gamer system -- except hers is 'DOKI DOKI LOVE CURE,' an otome dating sim that tracks Tsundere, Dandere, Yandere, and Kuudere route points. The system locks onto Motoko as the romance target, and within a day the entire crew knows about Hiromi's feelings.

94771117.title: Kodokuna Kōkaku Kidōtai Route Pt.2
summary: A week into Hiromi's otome system nightmare, the crew has figured out her crush on Motoko. The Doki Doki Love Cure tracks her route points daily -- Tsundere 48, Yandere 32 -- while Hiromi wearily dismisses the morning notification and tries to maintain her dignity around the oblivious object of her affection.

94771258.title: A Series of Unfortunate Perks
summary: Continuation of Hiromi's Doki Doki Love Cure ordeal, with Challenge Mode preventing save-scumming. The otome system keeps awarding route points for interactions Hiromi can't control while she desperately tries to play it cool.

94771336.title: Fixers are just car salesman
summary: Fixer Sebastian Ibarra -- an ex-basketball kid from the wrong side of the tracks -- gets interrupted during a quiet moment of nostalgia. The story frames fixers as glorified car salesmen hustling edgerunners the same way dealers hustle marks.

94775182.title: WNS News report
summary: WNS News covers a protest at City Hall by the NC Scavengers Association, with spokesperson Pavel Andreyev demanding Mayor Rhyne stop the Tyger Claws and their hired merc from exterminating scavengers. The absurdity of organ harvesters demanding police protection hits peak Night City.

94807177.title: Wicked Maiden
summary: Mishka, a 140kg bear of a man smuggled from Vladivostok to harvest chrome for the Motherland, meets his handler Nikolai in a Night City warehouse. The scav operation gets a rude awakening when their newest target turns out to be connected to Motoko's hunting grounds.

94817419.title: MLRS
summary: NCPD officers spot Motoko's Quadra doing ten over the limit. The deputy identifies the vehicle-mounted weapon system as an MLRS -- Multiple Launch Rocket System. The cop decides the speeding ticket isn't worth getting turned into a crater.

94829206.title: Motoko the Loose cannon
summary: At a TC meeting, Boss Azegami orders Motoko to drop her grudge against the Maelstrom. Motoko's hackles rise at being told what to do by a man she doesn't answer to -- the tension between her independence and the TC hierarchy reaches a breaking point over who gets to decide when violence ends.

94830013.title: Fight song
summary: The TC prepares for the second Maelstrom war. Motoko inspects her Nekomata rifle and Burya pistol as Jun calls positions over comms. She's posted on the roof of a TC casino that Maelstrom plans to hit, ready to turn the defense into a slaughter.

94830388.title: UR'S ANGELS, PART I
summary: Duplicate of entry 403 -- the second Maelstrom war defense at the TC casino.

94837621.title: Motoko has a thing for Yandere.
summary: Motoko patrols Night City hunting cyberpsychos. She encounters one whose fractured mind and obsessive love remind her uncomfortably of a yandere archetype -- raising the question of whether her fascination with saving these broken chrome addicts says something about her own psychology.

94840564.title: Maelstrom naming convention
summary: Duplicate of entry 405 -- Motoko's cyberpsycho patrol with yandere observations.

94965514.title: Burrito starved
summary: With no XXL burrito machine outside the new apartment, Jun orders a TC gonk to fetch three burritos from a machine that's literally three feet away. The gonk complies instantly -- self-preservation means obeying the Oni without question, even for absurd errands.

94965721.title: The intervention of Jun
summary: Duplicate of entry 408 -- Jun sends a TC gonk on a burrito run.

94966927.title: Respect
summary: Motoko catches TC gangers hassling Malcolm and Ichi in the garage while they help with the move. The gangers don't know who she is, which infuriates her after scavs just targeted her home. She sets them straight with a combination of reputation, chrome, and the fact that Fujimura personally gave her the apartment on the 27th floor.

94968337.title: BOLO
summary: Motoko accuses Jun of getting the apartment from Fujimura before even asking her to move. She counters that she built her own mobile home. Jun asks what she means by 'built' and 'mobile' -- and an hour later they're standing in the Badlands staring at something preem and terrifying.

94968454.title: Not the point Jun!
summary: Duplicate of entry 411 -- Motoko reveals her mobile home build to Jun in the Badlands.

94980025.title: Dough girl
summary: TC members Keiko, Masaki, and Shouma argue about the girl who moved into the 27th floor without wearing Tyger colors. Keiko calls bullshit on anyone disrespecting Fujimura's name like that, while Shouma points out that living on a floor reserved for TC brass probably means she has serious backing.

95018197.title: Got a little something
summary: Motoko walks out of her bedroom with something new on her forehead -- some kind of new chrome or implant. Jun points at it speechlessly while sipping his XXL Burrito coffee, and Motoko plays innocent.

95042386.title: Pokemon Trainer Motoko
summary: A crossover thought experiment: what if Motoko from Ghost in the City got reincarnated into the Pokemon universe as a New Game+? She'd be deeply uncomfortable being organic again, wildly overpowered from retained memories, and twice as unhinged as her first run.

95096354.title: Untitled Fluff Piece
summary: An alternate awakening scene: Hiromi wakes to someone stroking her hair and whispering 'morning sleepyhead.' The dream diverges from her usual one -- soft laughter, a warm voice, Motoko being gentle instead of chaotic. A tender what-if scenario of domestic peace between the two.

95105222.title: Fulton 'recover' device
summary: An analysis of why Motoko can't just burn out every scav in NC -- it's not about her own safety but protecting Jun, Hiromi, Jackie, and the rest. The real threat isn't gangs or corps but fixers who weaponize information, making anyone she cares about a potential leverage point.

95139587.title: Raid ensurance
summary: A fake TV commercial for 'Raid Insurance' -- a satirical ad targeting scavengers, promising coverage for when their den gets raided. The ad runs alongside a news segment listing TC scav-den sweep kill counts like lottery numbers, followed by Hiromi's excited text to Motoko about the broadcast.

95160344.title: Trauma team at home
summary: Hiromi suggests a Trauma Team membership for Section 9. Motoko says they have Trauma Team at home and produces a white duck wearing a stethoscope. Section 9's medical coverage is exactly as professional as Motoko's sense of humor.

95228360.title: Kiry-I mean Motoko-Chan!
summary: Commentary on Section 9's matching jackets and Motoko learning that gang members are actual people rather than a monolithic hostile entity. The realization that not every Tyger Claw is on board with human kidnapping lands harder when you live in their building.

95238845.title: Fujimuras True Plan
summary: Fujimura assembles a hand-picked team including Motoko for a secret mission. She despairs at getting pulled into TC business again -- until the 'mission' turns out to be something completely unexpected and non-violent, revealing Fujimura's true plan has nothing to do with gang warfare.

95274200.title: Pigeontinez
summary: Adam Smasher gets sent to clear cyberpigeons off an Arasaka research roof. The result: a brand-new crater, no building, and someone in Arasaka management screaming into a phone about proportional response.

95324695.title: No politics rule
summary: A pitch for how Phantom Liberty's spy thriller would mesh with GiTS-style investigative gameplay -- Motoko operating blind without meta-knowledge, forced to actually use her detective skills against foreign imperialist spies while navigating Night City's political underbelly.

95355712.title: Lost Tales of the Forest Clan
summary: A scene from the main story retold with emphasis on Motoko's acrobatics -- powersliding off a roof, cutting a wire, falling chrome-hands-first through the gap while studs rip from concrete above her. Kinetic action prose tracking every beat of an infiltration.

95411539.title: V is for Velocity
summary: V stews on a rooftop after the heist, angry at Jackie being hurt and Motoko's judgmental glaring. She mockingly imitates Motoko's 'I could knock this gig out by myself' attitude, then a digital glitch announces an unwelcome visitor -- forcing V to confront her new reality as a street merc.

95413618.title: THE MOTOSHOW!
summary: Duplicate of entry 425 -- V brooding on the rooftop post-heist.

95417548.title: Atom(Symbol) Smasher? Some gonk I'm sure.
summary: Rogue hits the bottle hard at the Afterlife after learning about Motoko's latest exploits. Claire the bartender watches Night City's greatest fixer drink herself into a stupor over a teenage merc who keeps rewriting the rules of the game.

95433520.title: Homework is important! *Activates Edgerunner team*
summary: Hiromi gets an Arasaka Academy assignment on brand recognition and competitor sabotage. She calls in Section 9 to handle it like an actual corporate operation -- because when your homework involves damaging rival brands, having an edgerunner team on speed dial is just good resource management.

95437054.title: Mississippi Custardgravy
summary: Duplicate of entry 428 -- Hiromi's brand recognition homework turned into a Section 9 operation.

95439832.title: Hiromi meets her Jounin-Sensei
summary: A breakdown of the Corporate role's 'Resources' stat from tabletop Cyberpunk, framing it as a lesson in how Hiromi's corporate training at Arasaka Academy translates into commanding bodyguards, weapons, buildings, and even hitmen -- essentially her Jounin-Sensei education in corporate warfare.

95451616.title: Never seen that before in my life!
summary: NCPD Captain John Smith pulls over Motoko's car and the officers start cataloguing contraband -- customized Burya, homemade ammo, homemade grenades, an escalating arsenal that makes each cop back up a step further. A routine traffic stop turns into an impromptu arms inspection.

95491543.title: Budget Cuts
summary: Jackie and V watch a MaxTac aerodyne fly overhead -- but instead of armored badges dropping out, a purple-haired teenager jumps twenty feet to the ground while a combat song blasts from the warning sirens. Motoko has somehow gotten herself deputized by MaxTac, or at least convinced them to let her ride along.

95523580.title: Life coaching and lack thereof.
summary: Jackie and Motoko stage an intervention for V's mental health. They've set up a 'healing plan' with Jackie's new input (Misty), but V already has a Quantified Satori life coach. Motoko and Jackie argue that corporate wellness programs abandon you when the eddies dry up.

95571022.title: Gonk density set to maximum
summary: Motoko realizes she and Hiromi might have been dating. Hiromi holds up matching wedding rings. Motoko insists those are friendship rings. Hiromi trots out their 2.5 children. Motoko's gonk density finally approaches critical mass.

95571856.title: It Runs In The Family.
summary: Jun hurries to finish his burrito before Motoko can steal it. A neighbor dragging musical equipment catches his attention, and he offers to help. The encounter reveals that Motoko's burrito-stealing reflexes have gotten so fast that Jun can no longer eat in peace -- a family trait of escalating chrome and diminishing snack security.

95747560.title: Making a friend!
summary: A veteran TC martial arts coach -- more ring fighter than street brawler -- watches a young student he's been mentoring struggle with humiliation after getting outclassed. The story explores mentorship within the Tyger Claws, where reputation means everything and a good teacher has to balance building skills with managing wounded pride.

95781721.title: "I'll gut you."
summary: Post-recovery, Motoko calls Fujimura to threaten him about forcing Jun to take another oath. He hangs up on her. She appears outside his third-story window holding a knife: 'I'll gut you.'

95785438.title: Edgerunners: Conflict
summary: Analysis of how a crew like Maine's Edgerunners would actually perform in combat -- Rebecca with twin SMGs could clear the same room as any elite solo, they have someone smart enough to case targets beforehand, and a decent netrunner. The piece argues the crew is more competent than the show implies.

95788573.title: Onryo
summary: Follow-up to the 'I'll gut you' scene, discussing Motoko's reputation as an Onryo -- a vengeful ghost -- in the eyes of those who cross her or her brother. The TC and other gangs treat her threats as promises.

95800627.title: IMOUTO HORRIBLIS III: RE:SOLUTION
summary: An alternate timeline where Maine's crew crosses paths with Jun and Motoko. Rebecca recognizes the Oni, connects the dots to Motoko, and stops Maine from executing Jun. Akari fights Dorio and Sasha in a brutal three-way chrome dance while Motoko and Rebecca size each other up -- two small, heavily-armed women recognizing a kindred spirit.

95858218.title: Super Ninja Mercenary Motoko strikes again! Nin nin!
summary: Arasaka Academy's Show and Tell becomes a corporate power play. Hiromi brings Motoko as her presentation, and the purple-haired merc turns the classroom into a demonstration of exactly why independent contractors outperform corporate assets.

95860003.title: Wait, this isn't take your Merc to class day.
summary: Motoko shows up uninvited at Arasaka Academy and runs into Hiromi between classes. The purple-haired merc navigating corpo school hallways creates an immediate culture clash -- street-level chaos meets corporate conformity, with Hiromi caught in the middle trying to keep things from escalating.

95868226.title: The Sunrise
summary: A techie called Gearslot works long hours fabricating heat sinks, trying to distract himself from something. He's not a fighter by nature, but recent events forced him to chrome up with combat mods. The story follows a blue-collar Night City worker processing trauma through the only therapy available -- keeping busy and staying useful.

95870617.title: Uploading homework for fun and profit
summary: Duplicate of entry 443 -- Gearslot the techie working through trauma.

95875060.title: Rache Bartmoss had almost entirely no links to this omake
summary: Hiromi gives a class presentation at Arasaka Academy using heavy redaction and corporate doublespeak. Motoko crashes the session by killing the lights, flashing a powerpoint slide reading 'You saw nothing! Hiromi is the best. Rache Bartmoss had almost entirely no links to this presentation,' then vanishes down the hallway giggling, leaving model Lexington pistols on every desk.

95878603.title: every Section 9 needs a togusa
summary: Duplicate of entry 445 -- Hiromi's redacted class presentation with Motoko's guerrilla PowerPoint attack.

95882470.title: Oh shi
summary: A kid steals grenades from an open car and hides in a pay-to-flush toilet to examine his loot. The resulting explosion takes out the building's water feed and makes the news as a 'terrorist toilet bombing' -- collateral chaos from Motoko leaving ordnance in her unlocked vehicle.

95884180.title: Low-key, a little offended
summary: Discussion of Rebecca's possible Mox connection through her neck tattoo resembling the Lizzie mascot labrys, plus speculation about a bad-end timeline where Jun dies and Motoko goes full cyberpsycho -- hunting Maine's crew and chroming up to maximum at Vik's.

95894848.title: Calculation failed
summary: Motoko finds a bodypillow of herself in Hiromi's bedroom. She assumes it's a joke collectible and asks where the rest of the Section 9 pillow set is. Hiromi ignores the question and asks Motoko how much she weighs -- trying to calculate her density for the custom pillow.

95896231.title: killing in the name (Of Fun!)
summary: Motoko finds the bodypillow and Hiromi frantically tries to explain. Motoko being Motoko, completely misreads the situation as a joke and asks if there's a full Section 9 set. Hiromi, sweating bullets, pivots to asking about Motoko's weight to calculate chrome density.

95924002.title: Malcolm's Nightmare...
summary: Malcolm watches in horror as Motoko mods his beloved Caliburn with chainguns, missiles, and armored plating. He wakes from a nightmare of Motoko approaching him with a toolbox and saw, only to find the real Motoko working on his car while Hiromi happily gets her bike upgraded.

95925616.title: Tinfoil protection
summary: Motoko tries and fails to get Jun to exercise, then hands him a tinfoil hat as his 'perfect firewall' when he begs for netrunner protection. Jun remains blissfully unaware that the hat is worthless.

95934662.title: Tin foil hat... Infinite protection
summary: Motoko slaps a tinfoil hat on Jun as a joke firewall, then notices she gained 10,000 Technical Ability XP. Jun rides NCART through Sixth Street turf and finds the hat actually works -- repelling picksockets and blocking malware while he pirates all ten Bushido movies. Hiromi calls to scream about the business opportunity after Fujimura offers 500 million eddies for a thousand units.

95934836.title: That's your imagination
summary: A salesman pitches tinfoil hat 'firewalls' in different corporate editions -- Manager, Executive -- while the buyer points out they look like Mickey Mouse ears and dunce caps.

95968886.title: Becca is sensitive, you know?
summary: Rebecca and Sasha go undercover in Japantown on a favor for Gloria Martinez, trying to dig up intel on Section 9 and what Gloria's kid David has gotten into. They chat up Jun at a bar but the Oni refuses to give up deets on his sister's crew. Becca keeps a hand on her Omaha as they leave Tyger Claw turf.

95975288.title: No mistake has ever happened copying Bushido 4. 5 on the other hand? Blegh.
